title: Explore first-run coach mark for connection gesture
description:
Exploratory bead, not committed. Paul has not decided whether to ship a first-run coach mark teaching the new connection gesture from contextflow-twa (GH #22, draw.io-style hover stubs + whole-shape drop). Filing so the idea is not lost while the implemented stubs are evaluated in real workshop use.

Concept: a one-shot floating callout anchored to a stub on the first context a user hovers, with a short message teaching the drag gesture, dismissable via "Got it" button or auto-dismissed on first successful relationship creation. Persisted via localStorage. Reset surface for workshop facilitators.

Mockup: docs/mockups/connection-ux-mockup.html, Panel 4, on branch mockup/connection-ux. Demonstrates the proposed placement, dismissal, and replay/reset.

Proposed details (all up for revision):
- Trigger: project has >= 2 contexts AND 0 relationships AND user hovers any context for the first time.
- Anchor: floating callout beside the source card's right stub, with a pointer triangle. Re-anchors if the card moves.
- Wording: "Drag any arrow from this context to another to map the relationship. After releasing, pick a DDD pattern." Direction-neutral, consistent with contextflow-twa's tooltip wording.
- Dismissal: "Got it" button, OR auto-dismiss on first relationship creation.
- Persistence: localStorage.contextflow_coach_connection_seen = true.
- Reset: a control under Help / Workshop Mode (location TBD) that clears the flag for fresh demos.
- Visual register: matches GettingStartedGuideModal (slate body, blue accent, no emojis).

Open questions before committing:
- Does it add real value, or does the affordance (hover-revealed arrow stubs + per-stub tooltip + updated rich tooltip) already teach itself? Evaluate after contextflow-twa ships and gets some workshop or self-use time.
- Is anchoring to one stub the right placement, or should the callout sit elsewhere (above the card, in a canvas corner with an arrow drawn to the affordance, etc.)?
- Should the trigger include "user has at least seen one example project" so brand-new users meet the welcome guide first, then the coach mark on their own project?
- Coach mark family: this is the first one; if accepted, does ContextFlow grow a small coach-mark system (CoachMark.tsx component, useCoachMark hook) for future first-run teaches?

Acceptance criteria are not finalized -- decide first whether to ship at all. If yes, work this bead by writing acceptance criteria from the chosen behavior, then implement.

Depends on contextflow-twa (the affordance must exist before there is anything to teach).

title: Add explicit "finding" state(s) to Boundary / Role / Code Size / Ownership pills (terminology TBD)
description:
Follow-on to contextflow-l4i. The redesigned PillGroup currently treats "no pill selected" as the only unset state, which conflates two distinct domain meanings: (a) "unassessed" (we have not looked at this yet, the default for any new context) and (b) an explicit, deliberate finding (we looked, and the finding itself is meaningful: unknown, disputed, mixed, unowned, etc.).

Workshop facilitators surfaced this as a real distinction. The non-set state is not a missing-data placeholder, it is a finding. The whole point of context mapping is to surface exactly this kind of ambiguity, and the tool should let teams name it.

## Goal

Introduce one or more explicit "finding" enum values as first-class options for the four pill fields, distinct from "not assessed yet." Treat selecting a finding as a deliberate workshop output worth tracking.

## Open question 1: per-field terminology

"Unknown" is a generic placeholder. Each field surfaces a different kind of finding, and the vocabulary should match. Decide before implementation, ideally with facilitator input.

Working candidates (not final, brainstorm only):
- Boundary Integrity: Unknown / Unclear / Variable (different parts of the boundary differ) / Disputed
- Code Size: Unmeasured / Opaque (legacy black-box) / Not Sized
- Business Model Role: Mixed / Multi-role / Ambiguous / Unmapped
- Ownership: Unowned / Disputed / Shared (multiple teams touch without a clear primary) / Unknown

Some fields may want multiple non-set pills (e.g. Ownership could legitimately have both "Unowned" and "Disputed" as distinct findings). That decision blocks the rest of implementation, so settle it first.

## Open question 2: canvas rendering

1. Boundary=finding: distinct treatment (e.g. dashed border) or fall back to the "unset" default? Recommend distinct so the canvas reflects the finding.
2. Code Size=finding: distinct node size or fall back to Medium? Recommend a small visual marker (e.g. question-mark badge on the node) over a size change, so node size keeps its current meaning.
3. Ownership=finding rendering: today ownership=external triggers a dotted ring. Should ownership findings get their own visual treatment? Recommend yes, since the whole point is to surface it.
4. Whether the canvas should have a "filter / highlight contexts with any finding state" affordance. Out of scope for this bead, worth a follow-on.

## Open question 3: scope split

Given the size, consider splitting into:
- Discovery spike: settle terminology with a facilitator and write up the per-field vocabulary
- Implementation bead per field, or one combined bead once vocabulary is fixed
- Separate bead for canvas rendering changes

## Scope (assuming the bead stays combined)

All four fields get at least one "finding" pill (final value/label TBD per question 1):
- Boundary Integrity
- Code Size bucket
- Business Model Role
- Ownership

Default state for new contexts remains undefined (unassessed). The finding state must be a deliberate user click, never an automatic fallback.

## Implementation surface (rough, depends on question 1 outcome)

- src/model/types.ts: extend the relevant union types with the new value(s)
- src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts: add tooltip content entries for each new value
- src/model/sync/schema.ts and src/model/sync/contextSync.ts and src/model/sync/contextMutations.ts: round-trip the new value(s) through Yjs (per the project rule that ALL entity CRUD goes through the collab mutation layer)
- src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx: add the new pill(s) to each affected pill group; reconsider grid layouts when pill counts change
- src/components/canvas (wherever the affected fields are read for rendering): handle the new case(s) per question 2 decisions
- src/lib/canvasConstants.ts: revisit only if a code size finding warrants a NODE_SIZES entry
- src/utils/analytics.ts: capture the new value(s) in tracked property changes
- Tests: extend ContextInspector tests; add sync round-trip tests for each new enum value
- Mutation testing: rerun against the touched sync mutations

## Acceptance criteria (high-level, sharpen after question 1)

1. User can select a deliberate "finding" state on Boundary, Code Size, Role, and Ownership using terminology that fits each field.
2. Findings persist through save/load, Yjs sync, and export/import (round-trip cleanly).
3. Findings are visually distinct from "unset" in the inspector (a pill is highlighted).
4. Canvas rendering for the new states follows the design decisions made in question 2.
5. Analytics events distinguish "user set value to a finding" from "user cleared the value."
6. Existing acceptance criteria from contextflow-l4i still hold (Boundary Notes textarea visibility, clearing behavior, etc.).
7. Default state for any new context is still undefined (unassessed), not a finding.

## Out of scope

- Keyboard navigation / ARIA for the new pill (covered by contextflow-813, will inherit)
- Per-pill tooltips for the new value(s) (covered by contextflow-939, will need to add entries once terminology is fixed)
- Filter / highlight view for contexts with finding states (separate follow-on)

## References

- contextflow-l4i (the redesign this builds on)
- contextflow-813 (keyboard nav, related)
- contextflow-939 (per-pill tooltips, related)
notes:
## Discovery prep-work (do before implementation)

Paul plans to write a blog post / LinkedIn post that explores the distinction between "unassessed" and explicit findings (unknown / unclear / unowned / disputed / mixed) on each of the four pill fields. The post serves as the discovery spike:

- Forces articulation of why each finding matters as a workshop output, not a missing-data placeholder
- Surfaces reactions from the DDD / context-mapping community, which usually has stronger vocabulary than I would propose in isolation
- Lets per-field terminology settle through public conversation before locking it into code, schema, and analytics

Do NOT start implementation until the post has run and the vocabulary has been chosen. The bead stays open as a parking spot; the post is the unblocker.

title: Slice 4: direction swap via picker mini-diagram (eta)
description:
Slice 4 of contextflow-ki1. User behavior: when viewing a U/D relationship in the inspector, the user sees a mini-diagram showing the two contexts and the arrow direction. Clicking the arrow flips the direction. The Direction section is hidden for Mutually Dependent relationships (Partnership has no direction).

Flip behavior (extends the existing swapRelationshipDirection store action):
- Per-side roles cleared on flip. If upstreamRole or downstreamRole were set, both are cleared and the indicator boxes disappear. Reason: per-side roles are claims about a specific context (e.g. "Billing has built an Open Host Service"), not slot labels, so they should not silently migrate to the other context when direction changes. The user re-asserts roles for the new direction.
- Customer-Supplier preserved on flip. If pattern === customer-supplier, the pattern is left untouched; the edge label still reads Customer-Supplier. Reason: C-S is a relationship-level pattern whose supplier/customer labels are defined by direction, so flipping just relabels who plays which role.
- Blank flip: a U/D relationship with no pattern and no roles simply reverses direction. Nothing to clear.

This is a semantic change to the swapRelationshipDirection action. All three flip surfaces (this picker mini-diagram, the canvas double-click in Slice 5, and the existing right-click context menu) share the action and therefore inherit the new behavior. That sharing is intended.

See docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md (Slice 4) for full acceptance criteria. See docs/mockups/two-sided-relationships-target.html for visual fidelity.
notes:
Implementer reminders:
- Analytics: direction flip (whether via picker, canvas, or right-click) fires a tracked event per docs/ANALYTICS_USAGE_GUIDE.md. The relationship_direction_swapped event already exists in store.ts; verify it still fires and consider adding a properties_changed entry listing upstreamRole/downstreamRole when they were cleared by the flip.
- The action change goes in src/model/store.ts swapRelationshipDirection. Today it only swaps fromContextId/toContextId; extend the same updateRelationship call to also set upstreamRole and downstreamRole to undefined. Pattern is not touched.
- Yjs sync: confirm that setting upstreamRole/downstreamRole to undefined round-trips correctly through the mutation layer (contextMutations or relationshipMutations).
- Tests: existing tests for swapRelationshipDirection assume role preservation; they need updating. The right-click context menu test in RelationshipEdge.test.tsx will also need updating since it shares the action.

title: Slice 5: double-click canvas arrow to flip direction (epsilon)
description:
Slice 5 of contextflow-ki1. User behavior: a user can double-click a relationship arrow on the canvas to flip its direction. Calls the same swapRelationshipDirection store action that Slice 4 extends.

Flip behavior (inherited from Slice 4):
- Per-side roles cleared on flip (claims about a specific context do not silently migrate).
- Customer-Supplier preserved on flip (relationship-level pattern, labels defined by direction).
- Blank U/D relationship simply reverses direction.

Single-click behavior is unchanged (still selects the relationship). Right-click Swap Direction is unchanged in behavior (third fallback) but inherits the new role-clearing since it shares the store action.

Edge cases:
- Double-click does nothing on Partnership relationships (no arrow rendered, no hit area target).
- Double-click does nothing on Shared Kernel relationships (no arrow line; SK is overlap-only).

Small change in src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx (add onDoubleClick to the hit-area path). The store action and ArrowLeftRight icon already exist; the role-clearing behavior is added in Slice 4 (contextflow-1w4). See docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md (Slice 5).

title: Arrow tip occluded by target context border
description: The relationship arrow tip lands exactly at the geometric box edge after #24, but the box border is drawn at that edge with non-zero stroke width, so the last 1-2px of the arrow point is hidden under the border. Most visible on external contexts (dashed ring) and on hover/selected states with thicker strokes. Fix options: (1) push the arrow tip out beyond the box edge by the target borders effective half-stroke-width, computed per node from rendered styles; (2) extend the arrow length and increase the path shortening by the same amount so the tip protrudes past the border with the marker base still meeting the path tail. Independent of GH #21 and #24; surfaces only after #24 made the tip land precisely at the edge.

title: Slice 6: drag context body to overlap creates Shared Kernel
description: Slice 6 of contextflow-ki1. User behavior: a user can create a Shared Kernel relationship by dragging one context's body until it overlaps another. If a relationship already exists between them, they are prompted to convert it. See docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md (Slice 6). Depends on contextflow-cqi (Shared Kernel as an entity with overlap rendering). The gesture in this slice produces the entity that cqi introduces.
notes:
Implementer reminders (from PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md Slice 6):
- Analytics: Shared Kernel creation by overlap, and conversion from existing relationship to SK (with the confirm), each fire tracked events per docs/ANALYTICS_USAGE_GUIDE.md.

title: Enrich store analytics metadata for delete/add events
description: The store's trackEvent calls for context_added, context_deleted, user_deleted, and user_need_deleted are missing useful metadata that the old (now-deleted) action files had. Add: context_type and ownership to context_added; relationship_count and group_count to context_deleted; connection_count to user_deleted; user_connection_count and context_connection_count to user_need_deleted. The project data is already in scope at each call site.

title: Track undo/redo usage
description:
The undo and redo actions in store.ts (lines 1030-1037) have no analytics. They delegate to Yjs UndoManager via getCollabUndoRedo().

Fix: Add undo_used and redo_used events. The Yjs UndoManager does not expose what action type was undone, so metadata is limited to a simple event fire. Do not attempt to track action_type or stack_depth; the underlying Yjs undo stack is opaque from the store layer.

This reveals how often users rely on undo/redo as a safety net.

title: Teams UI not surfacing in bounded context inspector
description:
CONFIRMED BUG. In a fresh project, there is no way to assign teams to bounded contexts.

The ContextInspector shows: Ownership (Our Team/Internal/External), Legacy, Big Ball of Mud, Role, Code size, Boundary, Notes, Issues, Relationships. No team assignment dropdown appears.

Additionally, the left sidebar with Repos/Teams tabs only appears when repos or teams already exist (e.g. in the ACME example project). In a new project, there is no sidebar and no way to create teams.

**Root cause investigation needed:**
- The team assignment dropdown code exists in ContextInspector.tsx (lines 207-234) but is not rendering
- Check if it's conditionally hidden when no teams exist in the project
- Check if the sidebar "Teams" tab with the "Add Team" button is hidden when the team count is 0
- The left sidebar (RepoSidebar/TeamSidebar) needs to be accessible even in empty projects so users can create their first team

**Key files:**
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx` lines 207-234 (team dropdown)
- `src/components/TeamSidebar.tsx` (team creation + sidebar tab)
- `src/components/RepoSidebar.tsx` (repo sidebar tab)
- `src/components/CanvasArea.tsx` (sidebar rendering logic)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Team assignment dropdown visible in ContextInspector when teams exist
- Sidebar accessible in empty projects so users can create teams
- Creating a team and assigning it to a context works end-to-end

GH issue: https://github.com/virtualgenius/contextflow/issues/4

title: Repository assignment UI not visible in bounded context inspector
description:
CONFIRMED BUG. In a fresh project, there is no way to assign repos to bounded contexts.

The ContextInspector does not show any repo assignment section. The left sidebar with Repos/Teams tabs only appears when repos or teams already exist. In a new project, there is no sidebar and no way to create or assign repos.

Repo assignment works via drag-and-drop from the RepoSidebar in projects that already have repos (e.g. ACME example), but there's no way to get to the RepoSidebar in a fresh project.

**Root cause:** Same as contextflow-4is. The sidebar is conditionally hidden when no repos/teams exist, creating a chicken-and-egg problem: you can't create repos because the sidebar that lets you create repos isn't visible.

**Key files:**
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx` (repo display section)
- `src/components/RepoSidebar.tsx` (repo creation + drag-and-drop)
- `src/components/CanvasArea.tsx` (sidebar rendering logic)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Sidebar accessible in empty projects so users can create repos
- Repo assignment via drag-and-drop works end-to-end from fresh project
- Assigned repos visible in ContextInspector

GH issue: https://github.com/virtualgenius/contextflow/issues/5

title: [a11y] Remove-from-group X button is focusable while invisible (opacity-0)
description:
## Symptom

In the Context inspector Groups section, each member-group chip has a "remove from group" X button that uses `opacity-0 group-hover/chip:opacity-100` (visible only when the chip is hovered). The button still participates in the tab order, so keyboard users can land on a button they cannot see.

## Source

`src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx` around line 396:

```tsx
<button
  onClick={() => removeContextFromGroup(group.id, context.id)}
  className="opacity-0 group-hover/chip:opacity-100 transition-opacity hover:bg-white/50 dark:hover:bg-black/20 rounded p-0.5"
>
  <X size={10} />
</button>
```

## Expected

The X button should be accessible to keyboard users without leaving them on an invisible focus target. Two reasonable approaches:

1. Add `focus-within:opacity-100` (and matching focus-visible classes on the button) so the chip reveals the X when any child receives focus. Keyboard parity with mouse hover.
2. Promote the X to always-visible (subtler styling, e.g. lower opacity at rest) so it is never hidden from keyboard or mouse users.

Option 1 keeps the visual restraint at rest and matches the existing hover model. Recommend it.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Tabbing onto the X button makes it visible (via focus-within on the chip, or by always being visible).
2. The X is removable from the keyboard (Enter/Space activates it, focus then moves to the next focusable element).
3. Mouse hover behavior is unchanged.
4. No regression in chip layout when no element is focused or hovered.

## Out of scope

- Redesigning the chip itself.
- Other inspector a11y issues (file separately).

title: Add keyboard navigation and ARIA roles to PillGroup
description:
Make the PillGroup component (introduced in contextflow-l4i) keyboard accessible via the WAI-ARIA radiogroup pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/radio/).

## PillGroup API change

Field label must receive an id so the pill group can reference it via aria-labelledby. Current ContextInspector field labels are unidentified spans; add an id (e.g. derived from the field name) and pass it to PillGroup as labelId.

Extend PillGroup props:
- labelId: string (id of the field label element, for aria-labelledby)

## Roving tabindex

Within a PillGroup, only one pill is tabbable at a time (tabindex=0); the rest are tabindex=-1. The tabbable pill is the currently selected one (or the first pill when nothing is selected). Tab moves focus into / out of the group; arrow keys move within.

## Container and pill attributes

- Container: role="radiogroup", aria-labelledby={labelId}
- Each pill: role="radio", aria-checked={value === optionValue}, tabindex per roving rule above, type="button"

## Keyboard interaction

- ArrowRight / ArrowDown: move focus to and select the next pill (wraps to first)
- ArrowLeft / ArrowUp: move focus to and select the previous pill (wraps to last)
- Home: focus and select the first pill
- End: focus and select the last pill
- Space / Enter: select the currently focused pill (no-op if already selected)
- Selection follows focus on arrow keys (per the radiogroup pattern), so onChange fires as the user navigates

Mouse click behavior is unchanged: clicking a pill selects it and moves focus to it.

## Focus indicator

Visible focus ring that is visually distinct from the active state. Use Tailwind focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-500 dark:focus-visible:ring-blue-400 focus-visible:ring-offset-1 on each pill.

## Acceptance criteria (observable)

A reviewer with only a keyboard (no mouse) can:
1. Tab into a PillGroup; focus lands on the currently selected pill (or the first pill if none is selected).
2. Press ArrowRight; focus moves to the next pill AND that pill becomes selected (onChange fires, the context updates).
3. Press ArrowRight from the last pill; focus wraps to the first pill.
4. Press Home; focus jumps to and selects the first pill. Press End; same for the last pill.
5. Tab again; focus leaves the PillGroup entirely (does not cycle through remaining pills).
6. With focus on a pill, a focus ring is visible and visually distinguishable from the active (selected) ring.
7. A screen reader (VoiceOver or NVDA) announces the field label, the radiogroup role, and the checked state of the focused pill ("Boundary, radio group, Moderate, selected, 2 of 3").
8. Mouse clicks continue to work identically to before.

## Scope

PillGroup component in src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx and the four ContextInspector callers (Ownership, Role, Code Size, Boundary).

## Out of scope

Keyboard nav for other inspector controls (Team dropdown, toggles, badges); ARIA improvements outside PillGroup; reworking the Team dropdown.

Depends on contextflow-l4i (PillGroup must exist first).

title: Add close button to all details/overlay panels missing one
description: Several details and overlay panels lack an explicit close affordance (X button), forcing users to click outside or guess at dismissal. Audit and add a consistent close control to every floating/overlay panel that does not already have one. Known candidates to verify: View Options panel, Context Details (Inspector), Relationship panel, any other side panels or popovers. Acceptance: every dismissable panel has a visible close button in a consistent position (top-right), keyboard-dismissable (Esc), with the same icon and hover treatment. Out of scope: redesigning panel layout (see contextflow-l4i for Inspector redesign).

title: Per-pill tooltips explaining each enum value
description:
Add hover tooltips to individual pills in PillGroup so users see what each enum value means without consulting external docs. Especially useful for Role (Revenue Generator vs Engagement Creator) and Boundary (what makes a boundary weak vs strong).

This is additive to the field-level help icon preserved by contextflow-l4i; pill tooltips appear on per-pill hover, while the field-level icon shows the concept overview.

## PillGroup API change

Extend the PillGroup option shape (introduced in contextflow-l4i) to accept an optional description:

  options: array of { value, label, adornment?, description?: string | ConceptDefinition }

When description is a ConceptDefinition, wrap the pill in InfoTooltip; when a plain string, wrap in SimpleTooltip; when omitted, no tooltip. Tooltip conventions per CLAUDE.md (no native title attribute).

## Reuse existing ConceptDefinitions

Use the existing per-option records in src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts where available:
- OWNERSHIP_DEFINITIONS (per-option ConceptDefinition record) -> use InfoTooltip per pill
- BOUNDARY_INTEGRITY (per-option ConceptDefinition record) -> use InfoTooltip per pill

For fields without per-option records, the worker has two choices:
(a) Add new per-option ConceptDefinition records to conceptDefinitions.ts (preferred for consistency):
    - BUSINESS_MODEL_ROLE_OPTIONS: Record<string, ConceptDefinition> with entries for revenue-generator, engagement-creator, compliance-enforcer, cost-reduction
    - CODE_SIZE_OPTIONS: Record<string, ConceptDefinition> with entries for tiny / small / medium / large / huge; LOC ranges should be sourced from the existing CODE_SIZE_TIERS concept body (do not invent new ranges)
(b) Pass plain strings to SimpleTooltip; less consistent with the existing pattern but acceptable if option-level concept content does not yet exist in the design system.

Recommend (a) for Role and Code Size to match the per-option pattern already used by Ownership and Boundary.

## Acceptance criteria (observable)

A reviewer opens the inspector for a populated context and:
1. Hovers each pill in the Ownership, Role, Code Size, and Boundary fields; a tooltip appears for each, distinct per option.
2. Tooltip content for Ownership and Boundary matches the existing per-option entries in OWNERSHIP_DEFINITIONS and BOUNDARY_INTEGRITY (no text duplication).
3. Tooltip content for Code Size mentions LOC ranges consistent with CODE_SIZE_TIERS (no contradictions).
4. Hovering the field-level help icon (preserved by l4i) still shows the field-level concept tooltip, distinct from per-pill tooltips.
5. Tooltips do not show via the browsers native title attribute (per CLAUDE.md tooltip conventions).
6. Pills without a description prop render with no tooltip (backwards compatible).

## Scope

PillGroup option API extension; per-pill tooltip wiring in ContextInspector for the four pill fields; optionally adding two new per-option ConceptDefinition records to conceptDefinitions.ts.

## Out of scope

Tooltips on other inspector controls (toggles, badges, Team dropdown); rewriting InfoTooltip / SimpleTooltip components; adding per-option tooltips to badges (Classification, Evolution).

Depends on contextflow-l4i (PillGroup must exist first).

title: Track connection deletion events
description:
Connection creation events are tracked (user_need_connection_created, need_context_connection_created) but deletions are not.

Store actions with no analytics (store.ts):
- deleteUserNeedConnection (line 808): just calls getCollabMutations().deleteUserNeedConnection()
- deleteNeedContextConnection (line 845): just calls getCollabMutations().deleteNeedContextConnection()

Fix: Add user_need_connection_deleted and need_context_connection_deleted events in the store actions. Include entity_type and entity_id metadata to match the creation pattern.

Note: userActions.ts has deleteUserNeedConnectionAction and deleteNeedContextConnectionAction functions, but the store does not use them; it calls collab mutations directly. The tracking should go in the store actions.

title: User projects: drag-to-move silently reverts and cloud sync never connects after page reload
description:
## Symptom

When the app boots with a user-created project (any project not in BUILT_IN_PROJECTS) as the active project from the previous session:

1. The cloud-sync icon shows "disconnected" with the tooltip "Cloud sync activates when you select a project," even though a project is plainly selected.
2. Dragging a context on the canvas appears to drop the node, then it snaps back to its original position. The change is silently rejected.
3. Other mutations (rename, edit, add, delete) also do nothing visible.

Switching to a different project and back, or creating a new project, makes everything work normally. So users running into this hit it on every page reload and have no clear indicator of what is wrong.

## Root cause: boot-order race in store initialization

`src/model/store.ts` at lines 1644-1655 starts collab mode based on a synchronous Zustand snapshot:

```ts
const initialState = useEditorStore.getState()
if (initialState.activeProjectId && initialState.projects[initialState.activeProjectId]) {
  ...
  initializeCollabMode(initialState.projects[...], { onProjectChange })
}
```

At that moment, Zustand has been seeded with `initialProjects` and `initialActiveProjectId` from `src/model/builtInProjects.ts`. Those values are computed at module-load time:

```ts
export const initialProjects = BUILT_IN_PROJECTS.reduce(...)   // built-ins ONLY
const storedProjectId = localStorage.getItem("contextflow.activeProjectId")
const storedProjectExistsLocally = storedProjectId && initialProjects[storedProjectId]
export const initialActiveProjectId = storedProjectExistsLocally ? storedProjectId : null
```

If the last-used project is a user project, the `storedProjectExistsLocally` check fails (it is not in the built-in map), so `initialActiveProjectId` is `null`. The boot guard at store.ts:1645 fails. `initializeCollabMode` is never called for this session.

`initializeBuiltInProjects(setState)` at store.ts:1641 *does* load user projects from IndexedDB asynchronously and hydrate Zustand later, but by then the boot block has already given up. Nothing kicks off collab mode after async hydration.

Downstream consequences:
- The module-level `collabStore` in `src/model/sync/useCollabMode.ts` stays `null`.
- `getCollabMutations()` returns methods that all call `collabStore?.xxx(...)` — silent no-ops.
- Every editor action that routes through `getCollabMutations()` (move, rename, edit, add, delete) gets dropped.
- Zustand state never updates from the drag handler, `baseNodes` recomputes from unchanged project state, the `useEffect` at CanvasArea.tsx:446 calls `setNodes(baseNodes)`, and the dragged node visually snaps back.
- The network path (`reconnectCollabForProject`) is also never called for the booted project, so `connectToProject` never fires, the cloud icon stays at `disconnected`, and the tooltip falsely implies the user has not selected a project.

## Why it does not happen for built-in projects

For a built-in project, `initialActiveProjectId` is non-null and `initialProjects` contains it synchronously, so the boot guard succeeds and `initializeCollabMode` (local-only) runs. That covers drag-to-move locally. But even for built-ins, the network path is never started at boot, so the cloud icon stays disconnected until the user switches projects.

## Proposed fix

Remove the synchronous boot block at store.ts:1644-1655 and move the work into `initializeBuiltInProjects`s `.then()` callback in `src/model/builtInProjects.ts:115`. After it builds the `projects` map and resolves the `activeProjectId`, if `activeProjectId` is set and points to a real project, call `reconnectCollabForProject(activeProjectId, projects[activeProjectId])`. That path already:

- destroys any prior collab store
- triggers `useCollabStore.connectToProject(projectId)` (the network WebSocket)
- populates the Yjs doc from the project
- calls `initializeCollabModeWithYDoc` with an `onProjectChange` that also autosaves to IndexedDB

That gives both the local Yjs sync (so mutations land and renders update) and the network connection (so the cloud icon flips to connected). One unified path, used by every other entry point already (`selectProject` store.ts:323, `createProject` :346, `createFromTemplate` :372, `loadSharedProject` :1434).

There is a small wrinkle: `reconnectCollabForProject` is currently a private function inside store.ts. Either export it for builtInProjects.ts to call, or pass it in as a callback when invoking `initializeBuiltInProjects`. The callback form keeps the dependency direction sane (store.ts already imports from builtInProjects, not the other way around).

## Acceptance criteria

1. User reloads the page on a user-created project. The cloud icon flips from disconnected to connecting and then to connected within a few seconds (matching what happens after a fresh `selectProject`).
2. User drags a context after reload. The move persists. Reloading again shows the new position.
3. User reloads on a built-in project. Same behavior as (1) and (2): cloud connects, drags persist.
4. Network worker is offline (intentionally kill wrangler dev). The user still sees the project, drags still persist locally (because local Yjs runs regardless), and the cloud icon goes through `reconnecting` then `offline` rather than silent failure.
5. No regression in the existing project-switching, project-create, or shared-project flows.

## Files in scope

- `src/model/store.ts` (remove the racy boot block, optionally export `reconnectCollabForProject` or accept it as a callback)
- `src/model/builtInProjects.ts` (call the unified connect path after async hydration)
- Tests for boot-time behavior, particularly:
  - `src/model/builtInProjects.test.ts` (extend with a case that asserts the connect path is invoked after hydration)
  - Possibly a new integration-style test that walks through "reload on a user project, then drag, then assert position is persisted"

## Notes

This issue is the underlying reason for the dev-loop frustration that led to `scripts/dev-local.sh`. The script makes startup deterministic, but it cannot mask an app-level startup bug. Both are worth keeping.

title: [bug] Context can be saved with null positions, breaking drag and relationship rendering
description:
## Symptom

A context can end up in IndexedDB with `positions.flow.x = null` and `positions.shared.y = null` (and `positions.strategic = {}`, `positions.distillation = {}`). Discovered on user project "Test" while verifying the contextflow-9s6 fix. The "Test" context had:

```json
{
  "positions": {
    "strategic": {},
    "flow": { "x": null },
    "distillation": {},
    "shared": { "y": null }
  }
}
```

Other 3 contexts in the same project had valid positions.

## Consequences

1. React Flow renders the node with empty `transform: ""` (no translate), so the node sits at viewport (0,0) but more importantly the drag system never initializes for it; the user cannot drag the context to fix the position from the UI.
2. Any relationship with this context as an endpoint renders with NaN coordinates, producing ~30 SVG console errors per render (`<path> attribute d: Expected number, "MNaN,NaN..."`, `<rect> attribute x: Expected length, "NaN"`, etc.) and invisible edges.
3. From the user's perspective: a context sits in the wrong place and all relationships involving it appear to have disappeared.

## How the data got here

Unknown. Suspects to investigate:

- `addContext` in store.ts always sets initial positions from `findFirstUnoccupiedFlowPosition` / `findFirstUnoccupiedGridPosition`, so a freshly-added context should not have null fields. Possibly a Yjs sync path that maps `undefined` to `null` (`yFlow.set('x', positions.flow.x)` when `x` is undefined).
- `migrateProject` in persistence.ts may set null defaults under some legacy schema branch.
- Import path from `.project.json` may not validate position completeness.
- A previous bug (now-fixed contextflow-9s6 had silent no-op mutations) may have left a partial write somewhere that re-deserialized as null.

## Proposed fix (two layers)

1. **Defensive read**: in `getContextCanvasPosition` (src/lib/positionUtils.ts) or in the React Flow node-builder (CanvasArea.tsx around line 232), treat null / undefined / NaN x or y as 0 explicitly, and log a warning so the bad data surfaces. Prevents the NaN cascade through edges.
2. **Migration**: in `migrateProject` (src/model/persistence.ts), detect any context with non-finite x or y in any view's positions and reset to a safe default (e.g. center 50,50). Logs the migration. This heals existing broken projects on load.

The root-cause write path should also be found and patched, but the defensive layers prevent user-visible breakage in the meantime.

## Repro

1. Use the live IndexedDB query: open a user project where any context has a null `positions.flow.x` and `positions.shared.y`. Reload.
2. Observe ~30 NaN SVG errors in the console.
3. Try to drag the affected context. React Flow does not initiate the drag.
4. Set valid positions via direct IndexedDB write (e.g. flow.x=50, shared.y=50, strategic.x=50, distillation={x:50,y:50}) and reload. Display recovers, drag works.

## Acceptance criteria

1. A context with null/undefined/NaN positions does not produce NaN SVG attribute errors.
2. A context with null positions is still draggable (React Flow gets a valid (x,y) and produces a working transform).
3. Loading a project that has the broken state migrates it to a safe default and the warning is logged once per migrated context.
4. New contexts continue to get sensible initial positions (no regression to `addContext`).
5. Tests cover `getContextCanvasPosition` with null/undefined/NaN inputs and `migrateProject` with the broken position shape.

## Out of scope

- Identifying the original write path (file a separate bead if the defensive fix + migration is sufficient and the source remains unknown).
- Other forms of data corruption (orphan relationships, etc.).

title: Audit and upgrade iconography across the app
description:
Replace remaining inline SVG icons with a consistent iconset. Project already depends on lucide-react (used in App.tsx, TopBar.tsx, ImportConflictDialog.tsx, TeamSidebar.tsx, KeyframeManager.tsx and elsewhere), but ~28 inline SVGs remain scattered across components (counted via grep "<svg" src/ -r).

Step 1: audit
- Inventory all remaining inline SVGs (grep "<svg" src/ -r) and categorize: navigation/action icons (plus, x, chevron, etc.), domain-specific icons (arrows for relationships, user/team glyphs), and decorative.
- For each inline SVG, identify the lucide-react equivalent. Note any gaps where lucide does not have a good match.

Step 2: decide
- Recommended default: consolidate on lucide-react (already a dependency, broad coverage, consistent stroke style). Replace inline SVGs with lucide equivalents.
- If audit reveals significant gaps for domain-specific icons (e.g., DDD relationship glyphs, ContextFlow-specific marks), evaluate either keeping those as custom inline SVGs in a dedicated src/components/icons/ module, or adopting a complementary iconset (phosphor-icons or @radix-ui/react-icons).

Step 3: replace
- Migrate inline SVGs to the chosen iconset
- Centralize any retained custom icons in src/components/icons/
- Ensure stroke width and size are consistent (lucide default stroke-width=2)
- Update mockups (docs/mockups/*.html, docs/inspector-panel-mockup.html) to use the same icons (or note the divergence)

Scope: visual consistency only. No behavioral changes.

Out of scope: rebranding, changing the visual identity, restyling icon containers.

Note: this may also touch the inspector redesign output from contextflow-l4i. Consider sequencing so l4i lands first to avoid merge friction.
close_reason: Replaced emoji icons with lucide-react across inspector badges, pills, toggles, temporal block, mutual relationships, node badges (legacy/BBoM), and App.tsx sidebar. Mockup at docs/mockups/iconography-mockup.html. Verified visually with playwright.

title: ARCHITECTURE.md: update data model to match types.ts
description:

- Missing metadata fields: version, isBuiltIn, createdAt, updatedAt

BoundedContext interface:

- Field name wrong: doc says 'label' but code uses 'name'

Other missing types:

- ContextOwnership type
- User, UserNeed, UserNeedConnection, NeedContextConnection interfaces (replacements for Actor/ActorConnection)

title: ACL and OHS boxes on the same context side overlap
description:
When a single bounded context plays both roles for relationships that resolve to the same side, the two indicator boxes render in the same position and overlap.

Concrete setup that triggers it:
- Context X is the downstream end of an ACL relationship with context A.
- Context X is the upstream end of an OHS relationship with context B.
- A and B are both positioned such that the indicator auto-relocation logic picks the same side of X for each (e.g., both relationships use X's right side).
- The ACL box hugs X on that side, and the OHS box hugs X on the same side. They overlap.

This is rare in workshop diagrams (most contexts only play one role per side), but it is reachable, and the current layout has no handling for the collision.

Scope notes:
- Affects only ACL/OHS indicator boxes, not the relationship lines themselves (those are routed independently per edge).
- Related but distinct from contextflow-ki1 (two-sided relationship pattern modeling). This bead is purely about box layout, not relationship semantics.
- Builds on the geometry refinement landed in contextflow-if3.

Possible approaches to discuss before implementing:
1. Stack the two boxes along the perpendicular to the attachment edge (e.g., ACL above OHS on a right-side attachment), with the relationship lines updated to hit each box's outer midpoint.
2. Nudge one of the indicator boxes to the next-best side of the same context (the next side that still faces the other endpoint reasonably).
3. Combine into a single dual-label indicator when the two relationships are between the same pair of contexts (this would also cover the two-sided pattern case at the layout layer, even if contextflow-ki1 has not modeled it yet).

Acceptance criteria (to refine during design):
- When two indicators land on the same side of the same context, both are visible without overlap.
- Both relationship lines still attach to the outer-edge midpoint of their respective box and leave perpendicular to that edge.
- Auto-relocation continues to work as either context is dragged; the collision-handling rule re-applies on each layout pass.

Mockup work likely needed before code (project convention: visual changes get HTML mockups in docs/mockups/ first).

title: Track view preference toggles
description:
Settings toggles give insight into how users customize their view but none are tracked.

Store actions with no analytics (store.ts):
- toggleShowGroups (line 855)
- toggleShowRelationships (line 861)
- toggleIssueLabels (line 867)
- toggleTeamLabels (line 873)
- toggleRelationshipLabels (line 879)
- toggleHelpTooltips (line 885)
- setGroupOpacity (line 902)
- setColorByMode (line 907)

Fix: Add a single view_preference_changed event in each action. Include preference_name (string enum) and new_value (boolean for toggles, string for colorByMode, number for opacity). These are all safe to track (no PII). Helps answer: which visual layers do users actually use?

title: Slice 1: per-side roles available in inspector
description: Slice 1 of contextflow-ki1 (the two-sided relationships rollout). User behavior: a user can set Open Host Service, Published Language, Conformist, or Anti-Corruption Layer as a per-side role on a relationship through the InspectorPanel. See docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md (Slice 1) for acceptance criteria as observable behavior, files likely affected, and what does NOT change in this slice. Target visual: docs/mockups/two-sided-relationships-target.html. This is the foundational slice; other slices stack on it.

title: Context node: name shifts down when Legacy / Big Ball of Mud / Issues / External flags are set
description:
## Symptom

The context node title position is inconsistent depending on flags:

- No flags set: the name "Order Management" sits flush with the top of the inner padding.
- Big Ball of Mud or Legacy set: the name is pushed ~20px lower, leaving a visible gap above it.
- Both Legacy and BBoM set: same downward shift; no further movement, but the inconsistency between the flagged and unflagged states is jarring.

When a workshop facilitator toggles Legacy or BBoM on a context, every other context in the canvas keeps its title flush with the top while the flagged one drops, drawing visual attention to the indent instead of the badge. The title should hold its position. Badges should overlay the top-right corner without changing the text layout.

## Source

`src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx:322-342` renders the context name with a conditional `marginTop`:

```ts
marginTop:
  context.ownership === "external"
    ? "20px"
    : context.isLegacy ||
        context.isBigBallOfMud ||
        (context.issues && context.issues.length > 0)
      ? "20px"
      : "0",
```

The badges themselves are absolutely positioned at `top: 4px, right: 4px` (Legacy at line 231-243, BBoM at 246-259, Issue indicators at 282-320, External at 261-280), so they do not push layout. The `marginTop` is a defensive choice to avoid the name colliding with the badges, but for the workshop use case the badges are small (~16px square) and only consume a few characters of horizontal space in the top-right corner of a node that is hundreds of px wide. The collision risk is low; the inconsistency is the bigger UX issue.

## Proposed fix

Remove the conditional `marginTop` on the name and rely on absolute-positioned badges to overlay the top-right corner. Where collision is a real risk (very long names, very narrow nodes), use `padding-right` on the name only when a badge is present, keeping the vertical baseline constant.

Specifically:
- Replace the `marginTop` conditional with a constant `marginTop: 0`.
- Optionally add a `paddingRight` only when a top-right badge is shown, sized to clear the badges (e.g. `28px` for one badge, `48px` for two, or a single value that always reserves enough space). This avoids text running under the icons in the most extreme cases.
- Keep the External pill (left side) as-is; it overlays the top-left and the name does not start near the left edge anyway (the node has inner padding).

## Acceptance criteria

1. Toggling Legacy on a context does not move the name.
2. Toggling Big Ball of Mud on a context does not move the name.
3. Toggling Issues (any severity) on a context does not move the name.
4. Marking a context as External does not move the name (current 20px push for the External pill should also be removed; the pill is short and sits in the top-left and does not warrant pushing the title).
5. With both Legacy and BBoM active simultaneously, the two badges still appear top-right without overlapping each other (current code already offsets the BBoM badge by 20px when Legacy is present; preserve that).
6. With a very long name, the name truncates with the existing ellipsis behavior; it does not overlap the icons, even when both Legacy and BBoM are set.
7. All three view modes (Flow, Strategic, Distillation) render consistently. Animation between flag-on and flag-off is not jarring.

## Files in scope

- `src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx` (the only file with the offending `marginTop`)
- `src/components/nodes/__tests__/ContextNode.dragdrop.test.tsx` (sanity check; may not need changes)
- A visual regression check: open a project with one flagged and one unflagged context side by side; confirm both titles are at the same vertical baseline.

## Out of scope

- Restyling the badges themselves.
- Changing the badge anchor (still top-right; top-left for External).
- Any inspector-side behavior.

title: Represent Shared Kernel as overlapping contexts (GH #23)
description: Visual notation change: Shared Kernel renders as two overlapping context shapes with the kernel in the intersection region (hatching/tint), no connecting line. Aligns with Eric Evans canonical context map notation. Resolves the open overlap question from #21 (overlap is reserved for Shared Kernel; the edge algorithm only handles non-overlapping cases). Open design question on creation UX: drag-to-overlap creates the relationship, vs. relationship-first with positioning hint. See GH #23.

title: Issue icons overlap context name in header
description:
## Problem

When a context has issue annotations enabled, the issue icons render in the header area and visually overlap the context name text. Screenshot from "Dome Safety" context: two warning-triangle icons sit on top of the "Dome Safety" label, making both harder to read.

## Expected

Issue icons should sit alongside the context name with enough separation that neither obscures the other, or wrap below if there is not enough horizontal room.

## Files likely in scope

- `src/components/ContextNode.tsx` (header layout, icon row)
- Wherever issue icons are rendered in the node header

## Acceptance

1. Issue icons do not visually overlap the context name at default zoom for the common case (1-3 icons, short-to-medium name).
2. If both cannot fit on one line, icons wrap or move to a second row rather than overlapping the name.
3. No regression in nodes that have no issue icons.

title: Add educational tooltips to remaining Context inspector titles and section headers
description:
## Goal

After contextflow-l4i, three field labels in the Context inspector have the educational help-icon-next-to-label pattern (Role, Code Size, Boundary). Most of the other titles in the panel do not, even though many of them correspond to first-class DDD / context-mapping concepts that would benefit from a hover explanation. Add the same `InfoTooltip` + `HelpCircle` pattern to the rest.

This is the same idiom used today at `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx` for Role (line ~240), Code Size (line ~401), and Boundary (line ~415). Reuse the existing `FieldLabel` helper added in that bead.

## Scope: which titles need a tooltip

Field labels with no tooltip today:
- **Domain** (the mini-label above the Classification badge) — explains Core / Supporting / Generic
- **Evolution** (the mini-label above the Evolution badge) — explains Genesis / Custom-Built / Product / Commodity (Wardley)
- **Ownership** — note that l4i intentionally deferred per-pill tooltips to contextflow-939, but a field-level tooltip explaining the *concept* of ownership in a context map is still useful and is in scope here
- **Team** — what it means to assign a team to a context; relationship to Team Topologies if relevant
- **Groups** — capability clusters; how groups differ from individual contexts

Section headers with no tooltip today:
- **Strategic Profile** — what this group of fields tells you
- **Team & Organization**
- **Codebase**
- **Notes & Issues**
- **Relationships** — already has `POWER_DYNAMICS` tooltip on the header; keep as the reference example. No action needed.

Sub-section labels inside Notes & Issues:
- **Notes**
- **Issues**

Other panel elements out of scope:
- Name input (no separate label)
- Purpose textarea (no separate label; the placeholder explains it)
- Connected Users / Teams display under Purpose (these are not labels, they are read-only lists)
- Danger Zone (the Delete Context button is self-explanatory)

## What "educational tooltip" means here

Each tooltip should be a `ConceptDefinition` shown via `InfoTooltip`, matching the existing entries in `src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts` (BUSINESS_MODEL_ROLE, CODE_SIZE_TIERS, BOUNDARY_INTEGRITY, POWER_DYNAMICS, etc.). Content should be one to three short sentences naming the concept and explaining why it matters in a context map.

## Implementation

1. In `src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts`, add new `ConceptDefinition` entries for any concept we do not already have content for:
   - STRATEGIC_CLASSIFICATIONS exists for Core/Supporting/Generic; the new label-level Domain tooltip can reuse it
   - EVOLUTION_STAGES exists; reuse for the Evolution label-level tooltip
   - Add `OWNERSHIP_CONCEPT`, `TEAM_ASSIGNMENT`, `GROUPS_CONCEPT`, `NOTES`, `ISSUES`, plus one per section header
2. In `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx`, wire the new tooltips:
   - Add a `tooltip` prop to each `FieldLabel` call that does not have one yet (Domain, Evolution, Ownership, Team, Groups)
   - For section headers, extend `SectionDivider` (or wrap the label) so a section header can carry an `InfoTooltip` + `HelpCircle` the same way the Relationships header already does (see line ~540)
3. Update `src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx` if `SectionDivider`s label needs richer support (currently it accepts `React.ReactNode`, which already allows passing a flex row with an icon, so this may not require code changes there)

## Acceptance criteria

1. Hovering each label listed in Scope above shows an `InfoTooltip` with a clear, brief explanation, matching the existing Role / Code Size / Boundary affordance visually.
2. Tooltips respect the existing `showHelpTooltips` setting in the store (InfoTooltip already handles this).
3. Tooltip content is consistent in tone with existing entries in `src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts`. Defer wording review to a content pass if needed; do not block on perfect copy.
4. No new accessibility regressions: the help icon retains `cursor-help` and pointer hover behavior; keyboard focus on the icon shows the tooltip (matching current behavior).
5. Dark mode renders the new tooltips correctly.

## Relationships

- Builds on contextflow-l4i (which introduced the FieldLabel + tooltip pattern for Role/Code Size/Boundary).
- Adjacent to contextflow-939 (per-pill tooltips for individual enum values). 939 deepens the explanation per option; this bead is the field- and section-level layer above it.
- Adjacent to contextflow-pmt (header chrome tightening). If both land near each other, confirm the section header InfoTooltip is still visible after the chrome change.

## Out of scope

- Tooltips inside other inspectors (Group, User, Relationship, etc.). Worth a follow-on bead once the Context idiom settles.
- Restyling the tooltip component itself.
- Adding tooltips to canvas elements; this is panel-only.

title: Repos sidebar tab should show all repos, not just unassigned
description:
The Repos sidebar tab only appears when unassigned repos exist, but the Teams tab shows all teams regardless. Make Repos consistent: show all repos (assigned and unassigned) so the tab appears whenever any repos exist in the project.

## Current behavior
- `App.tsx:94-96`: `unassignedRepos` filters to `!r.contextId`
- `App.tsx:109`: `hasUnassignedRepos` gates the Repos tab visibility
- `App.tsx:284`: `RepoSidebar` receives only unassigned repos
- When all repos are assigned, the Repos tab disappears entirely
- Tab header says "Unassigned Repos (N)" in single-tab mode

## Required changes

### 1. App.tsx
- Add a `hasRepos` check: `(project?.repos?.length ?? 0) > 0`
- Replace `hasUnassignedRepos` with `hasRepos` in `hasLeftSidebarContent` and sidebar visibility logic
- Pass ALL repos to RepoSidebar: `repos={project?.repos || []}` instead of `repos={unassignedRepos}`
- Update auto-tab-switching logic (lines 115-120) to use `hasRepos` instead of `hasUnassignedRepos`
- Update tab labels: "Repos (N)" everywhere (drop "Unassigned" prefix)
- Keep `unassignedRepos` memo if needed for count display, or remove if no longer used

### 2. RepoSidebar.tsx
- Show assigned repos alongside unassigned ones
- Visually distinguish assigned repos: show which BoundedContext they belong to (e.g., a subtle label or chip with the context name)
- Assigned repos should NOT be draggable (they're already placed)
- Unassigned repos remain draggable for drag-and-drop assignment
- Consider grouping: unassigned repos first (ready to assign), then assigned repos below

### 3. Store / data access
- RepoSidebar will need access to context names to display assignments. Either:
  - Pass a `contexts` prop (or a `contextMap: Record<string, string>` of id->name), OR
  - Have RepoSidebar read from the store directly via `useEditorStore`
- Prefer the store approach to match TeamSidebar's pattern

## Edge cases
- Repo with contextId pointing to a deleted context: treat as unassigned
- External contexts: repos can't be assigned to them, but if somehow assigned, still show the assignment

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Repos tab appears whenever project has any repos (assigned or not)
- [ ] Unassigned repos are draggable; assigned repos are not
- [ ] Assigned repos show their context name
- [ ] Tab count reflects total repo count, not just unassigned
- [ ] Left sidebar auto-tab-switching works correctly with new logic
close_reason: Implemented: repos tab shows all repos, assigned repos display context name and are non-draggable, section headers when both types present

title: Add analytics tracking for untracked user actions
description:
Add trackEvent calls for high-value user actions that are currently untracked:

**High Value:**
1. Issue management (issue_added, issue_deleted, issue_updated with severity)
2. Import conflict resolution (track replace vs import-as-new choice)
3. Temporal navigation (keyframe_selected, timeline_date_changed)
4. Getting Started Guide opened (FTUE signal)
5. Keyboard Shortcuts modal opened (help-seeking behavior)

**Medium Value:**
6. Share dialog opened (compare with share_link_copied for drop-off)
7. CodeCohesion integration toggled (feature adoption)
8. Sidebar tab switches (repos vs teams panel usage)

Respect PII - no text content, no URLs, just event names and counts/types. Use TDD where the tracking logic involves conditional behavior (e.g., the issue management events that carry severity metadata).
close_reason: Added analytics tracking for 8 untracked user actions

title: Add Business Model Role field to bounded contexts
description: Add a businessModelRole dropdown to the BoundedContext entity and Inspector panel. Four options: revenue-generator, engagement-creator, compliance-enforcer, cost-reduction. This captures HOW a context contributes to the business, complementing the existing strategic classification (WHAT kind of domain). Include an InfoTooltip with educational content explaining each role, following the existing tooltip pattern (ConceptDefinition, respects showHelpTooltips). From DDD Crew Bounded Context Canvas V5.

title: Track share link generation
description:
The ShareProjectDialog component (src/components/ShareProjectDialog.tsx) lets users generate and copy collaboration links but has no analytics.

Key functions:
- getShareUrl (line 12): generates the share URL
- handleCopy (line 53): copies URL to clipboard

Fix: Add a single share_link_copied event in handleCopy. The share URL is generated on dialog open (not a separate user action), so tracking the copy is the meaningful signal. Include project metadata (pass project to trackEvent). Do NOT include the actual URL in the event (PII policy: no full URLs).

title: PR-trigger CI workflow so feature PRs aren't blocked by required build check
description:
PRs against main cannot merge through the normal flow because the only workflow that produces the required `build` status check (`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`) is triggered only on `push: branches: [main]`. Branch protection on main requires `build` to pass, but a PR branch never produces it.

Discovered while merging contextflow-bwn (Slice 1 of contextflow-ki1 / GH #13). Workaround used: fast-forward main to the slice commit and push directly, relying on the bypass-PR-allowances entry for paulrayner. GitHub then auto-closed the PR (#28) as merged because the SHA matched. This works but bypasses PR review entirely.

Future slice beads (h82, 7am, 1w4, 2v6, 384, hm4, jf0) will each ship as their own PR per the slice plan. Each will hit the same blocked state.

Suggested approaches (implementer picks one):

1. Split CI: add a `ci.yml` that runs on `pull_request` and runs typecheck + lint + build (no deploy), and keep `deploy.yml` push-only. The PR `build` check is satisfied by the new ci.yml job named `build`. Requires matching the job name to what branch protection looks for, or updating branch protection to require the new check name.

2. Add `pull_request` to deploy.yml's triggers but gate the deploy step on `github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`. Same job name `build`, runs on both, only deploys on push to main.

Either approach: verify on a throwaway PR before relying on it for a real slice merge.

title: Improve display of ownership info; decouple border style from external
description:
## Concern

Today external contexts get a dashed border and an orange fill. The current s0x mockup proposes a dashed border + slate fill instead. But baking "external" into the border style may be the wrong abstraction:

1. **Border style should arguably describe boundary integrity**, not ownership. A context with a leaky/undefined boundary feels conceptually different from a context that is well-defined but owned by someone else. Both could legitimately want their own border treatment, and the two ideas are independent.

2. **Ownership is already encoded by color** when `colorByMode === "ownership"`. In that mode, external contexts are orange, internal contexts are blue, ours is green. Adding a border style on top of color is redundant.

3. **Ownership signal becomes important when colors are by strategic classification** (core/supporting/generic). In that mode, ownership is otherwise invisible and needs its own signal.

## Investigate

- Should the border style be reserved for a separate axis (boundary integrity, well-defined vs ad-hoc, etc.) rather than ownership?
- When `colorByMode === "ownership"`, is the color enough? Can the external border style be dropped entirely in that mode?
- When `colorByMode === "strategic"`, what is the best non-color signal for ownership? A small ownership icon in the corner? A muted background tint independent of the strategic color? A label?
- Are there other contexts (no pun intended) where ownership matters: filtering, search, sharing diagrams with stakeholders?

## Related work

- `s0x` (context-node badge layout) ships the slate-dashed external treatment as a stopgap; this bead may revisit that decision.
- `colorByMode` toggle in store: `src/model/store.ts` + Settings panel.
- Boundary integrity concept: not currently modeled — may need a new field or this may be the catalyst for adding one.

## Out of scope (for this bead)

- Designing the boundary-integrity field or its UI. Note it for follow-up.
- Changing strategic classification colors.

## Acceptance

- A design proposal (mockup) covering ownership display in both color modes.
- A decision on whether border style stays tied to external or moves to a different concept.
- Either a confirmation that the slate-dashed treatment from s0x stays, or a follow-up bead to swap it out.

title: Track entity property update events
description:
Entity creation and deletion are well-tracked, but property updates are inconsistently covered. Context property changes ARE tracked via context_property_changed and trackTextFieldEdit in contextActions.ts. The same pattern should extend to other entity types.

Store actions with no update tracking:
- updateGroup (store.ts:512): group name, color, notes
- updateTeam (store.ts:615): team name
- updateUser (store.ts:696): user name, type (internal/external)
- updateUserNeed (store.ts:759): need text, visibility
- updateKeyframe (store.ts:1250): keyframe label, date
- updateFlowStage (store.ts:914): stage name, color
- updateUserNeedConnection (store.ts:813): connection properties
- updateNeedContextConnection (store.ts:850): connection properties

Implementation approach: Follow the contextActions.ts pattern:
- Use trackPropertyChange for enum/boolean changes (e.g., user type, need visibility)
- Use trackTextFieldEdit for text fields (PII-safe: char counts only, never actual text)
- Each update action receives an updates object; check which keys changed and track accordingly

PII rules apply: never track actual names, text content, or descriptions. Only track field_name, old/new char counts, and enum values.

title: Slice 2: edge indicators for per-side roles
description:
Slice 2 of contextflow-ki1. User behavior: a user sees a visual indicator on each context end showing the per-side role for that side. OHS and ACL match the existing app rendering (green and amber 28x18 boxes; see src/lib/canvasConstants.ts PATTERN_EDGE_INDICATORS). See docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md (Slice 2) for full acceptance criteria.

DESIGN DECISION (resolved 2026-05-23, see NOTES): Published Language (upstream) and Conformist (downstream) render NO on-canvas visual. Only OHS and ACL get indicator boxes. Ignore the dashed-placeholder treatment shown in the target mockup for PL/Conformist; render nothing on canvas for those.

Risk note: this is the slice v1 plan flagged as the hardest with the least detail. Worth a small geometry spike first, especially for the case where both OHS (upstream end) and ACL (downstream end) render on the same relationship. ACL/OHS box overlap collision when one context plays both roles on the same side is out of scope (contextflow-b6a).
notes:
RESOLVED open design decision (2026-05-23): Published Language (upstream) and Conformist (downstream) render NO on-canvas visual. The existing relationship-name label is the only on-canvas affordance for them.

Only OHS and ACL render indicator boxes (existing 28x18 green/amber per PATTERN_EDGE_INDICATORS in src/lib/canvasConstants.ts).

Reason: OHS and ACL are concrete code artifacts (a service, a translation layer) that deserve a visible badge; PL and Conformist are characterizations of how a side relates to the other, not code artifacts. Keeps signal-to-noise high at workshop zoom levels.

Resolution also recorded in docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md Slice 2 and the memory design_pl_conformist_no_canvas_visual.md.

Other implementer reminders:
- Reuse existing PATTERN_EDGE_INDICATORS config; do not introduce a parallel constant.
- Edge rendering lives in src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx and src/lib/canvasHelpers.ts (getEdgeLabelInfo).
- ACL/OHS overlap collision when one context plays both roles on the same side is out of scope (contextflow-b6a).
- Geometry spike recommended before implementation (two-box case especially).
