title: Set sensible canvas-overlay defaults so a newcomer sees a readable, not overwhelming, context map
description:
## Problem

Opening the ACME E-Commerce Platform sample (or any populated map) for the first time presents a wall of overlapping arrows, every one of them labeled with a DDD pattern term (Customer-Supplier, Conformist, Anti-Corruption Layer, Open-Host Service, Partnership, ...), plus colored capability-group blobs, plus issue annotations. For a workshop attendee or new user the result is overwhelming and the structure of the system does not read at a glance.

## Current defaults

`src/model/store.ts:152-173` defines the canvas-overlay toggle defaults:

| Toggle | Stored key | Default if unset |
|---|---|---|
| `showGroups` | `contextflow.showGroups` | true |
| `showRelationships` | `contextflow.showRelationships` | true |
| `showRelationshipLabels` | `contextflow.showRelationshipLabels` | true |
| `showIssueLabels` | `contextflow.showIssueLabels` | false |
| `showTeamLabels` | `contextflow.showTeamLabels` | false |

(Each one also honors a localStorage override if the user has toggled it before.)

The screenshot that motivated this bead shows issue labels turned on, which is the users own toggle from a prior session, not a default issue. The default of off is correct.

The actual problem default is `showRelationshipLabels: true`. Every arrow on a populated map gets a wordy pattern label, and on a dense map the labels overlap, repeat, and obscure both the arrows and the contexts. Workshop facilitators and DDD practitioners need them; first-time viewers do not.

## Proposed defaults

| Toggle | New default | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `showGroups` | true (unchanged) | Groups orient the viewer to capability clusters ("Customer-Facing Experience," "Platform Services," "Security & Compliance"). Removing them costs spatial structure. Keep on, but see follow-on suggestion below. |
| `showRelationships` | true (unchanged) | The whole point of a context map is the relationships. Without arrows the diagram is just a pile of boxes. |
| `showRelationshipLabels` | **false (changed)** | This is the single biggest source of first-impression noise. The pattern name on every arrow doubles the apparent item count on the canvas, and the DDD vocabulary is opaque to newcomers. |
| `showIssueLabels` | false (unchanged) | Issues are workshop output. The unflagged default is correct. |
| `showTeamLabels` | false (unchanged) | |

## Complementary change: hover / selection reveal

So power users do not lose information when `showRelationshipLabels` defaults off, surface the pattern label automatically for:
- the arrow currently under the mouse cursor (hover)
- the arrow currently selected (clicked)

This gives the "read one relationship at a time" affordance without the global label-on-every-arrow noise. Most canvas tools follow this pattern.

If implementing the hover/selection reveal is bigger than the toggle-default change, split into a separate bead and ship the default change first.

## Follow-on suggestion (optional, not blocking)

Groups currently render with a fairly saturated fill behind contexts. Even with `showGroups` on, lowering the default opacity (say from current ~0.15-0.20 alpha to ~0.08-0.10) would let the contexts breathe more on first read. Worth a quick visual A/B but not required by this bead.

## Acceptance criteria

1. A user opening any sample project (e.g. ACME E-Commerce, cbioportal) for the first time, with no localStorage overrides, sees arrows but no pattern labels.
2. Hovering an arrow shows that arrow s pattern label (and only that one). Clicking an arrow keeps the label visible while selected.
3. Toggling `showRelationshipLabels` on via the existing canvas controls restores the full-label behavior for every arrow (current behavior preserved).
4. The other four toggles default exactly as listed in the table above.
5. Users who have previously toggled `showRelationshipLabels` to a non-default value keep their preference (the existing localStorage-honors-stored-value path is unchanged).
6. No regression in Distillation View or Strategic View overlays.

## Files in scope

- `src/model/store.ts:152-173` (change the `showRelationshipLabels` default to false)
- Wherever the relationship label is rendered on the canvas: likely a relationship-edge component under `src/components/edges/`. Add hover and selection state handling so the label shows for the focused arrow even when the global toggle is off.
- `src/components/edges/__tests__/` (extend tests for the hover/selection-reveal behavior)

## Out of scope

- Restyling the labels themselves (font, color, position relative to arrow).
- Changing how groups render (color, opacity, opacity is a separate optional follow-on).
- Adding new toggles.

title: ACL/OHS indicator box: refine edge geometry, perpendicular tangent, and arrow placement
description:
Refine the geometry of relationship lines where they meet ACL/OHS indicator boxes. Interactive mockup: docs/mockups/acl-ohs-box-geometry-mockup.html.

CODEBASE CONTEXT (already in place, do NOT change unless noted):
- Indicator box rendering and ACL/OHS rendered path live in src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx. The path computation that feeds the rendered ACL/OHS paths sits in the ~100-160 range; the rendered aclOhsPath blocks (ACL branch, OHS branch) sit in the ~180-210 range; the indicator box <rect>+<text> rendering sits in the ~228+ range. Treat line numbers as orientation hints only.
- Indicator placement is driven by PATTERN_EDGE_INDICATORS in src/lib/canvasConstants.ts. Important convention used by this code: ACL uses position: "source", and the source end of the edge is the downstream context. OHS uses position: "target", and the target end of the edge is the upstream context. (Project rule: arrow always points to upstream, i.e., to the target end of the edge.)
- Existing helpers: getIndicatorBoxPosition (src/lib/edgeUtils.ts); getBoxEdgePoint, shortenEdgeEndpoint (src/lib/edgeGeometry.ts); getEdgeParams.
- Existing constants: EDGE_ENDPOINT_GAP = 6 (the small gap used at context-box attachments); ARROW_MARKER_LENGTH (marker size + gap).
- Indicator auto-relocation around the parent context as contexts are moved is ALREADY implemented (via getEdgeParams + getIndicatorBoxPosition). The mockup confirms this is the desired behavior; preserve it.

WHAT TO CHANGE (geometry rules, all visible in the mockup):
1. The indicator box hugs its parent context with a ~2px visual gap. No relationship line segment is drawn between the indicator box and its parent context.
2. The relationship line attaches to the outer edge of the indicator box: the side facing AWAY from the parent context. This is a locked rule, not "nearest edge to the other endpoint." (Mockup mode: "Proposed B: opposite from context.")
3. The attachment point is the midpoint of the chosen outer edge (vertical center for left/right edges; horizontal center for top/bottom).
4. The visible path is shortened so the tip sits in a small gap before the box border, using the same EDGE_ENDPOINT_GAP value already used at context-box attachments.
5. The curve style is a tangent-at-edge bezier at BOTH endpoints (the indicator-box end AND the non-indicator context end). At each endpoint, the bezier control point lies along the outward normal of the attachment edge at that endpoint, so the line leaves and enters perpendicular to that edge. Each endpoint gets its own control point, placed at a distance from that endpoint approximately equal to max(60, D * 0.5), where D is the Euclidean distance between the two attachment points. Strong enough that a clear perpendicular stub is visible at each end before the curve bends. Tune to match the mockup.
6. Do NOT use orthogonal/elbow paths. The project preserves a hand-drawn bezier aesthetic on relationship edges.
7. Arrow head placement: the arrow is always at the TARGET (upstream) end of the edge. Concretely:
   - ACL pattern (position: "source"): the target is the upstream context (the non-ACL context). The arrow sits at the end of the line that touches that context, pointing into the context. No arrow head appears at, or pointing at, the ACL box.
   - OHS pattern (position: "target"): the target is the upstream context, and the OHS box sits on it. The arrow sits at the OHS-box end of the line, pointing into the box.
8. Rules apply to ACL and OHS, on all four sides of the parent context (left, right, top, bottom).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (each item is observable canvas behavior; a worker should be able to write a failing scenario-level behavioral test from these alone):
- Given an ACL relationship is visible on the canvas, the ACL box appears flush against its parent (downstream) context with no relationship line drawn between them.
- Given an ACL relationship, the relationship line meets the ACL box at the midpoint of the outer edge of the box (the side facing away from the parent context), with a small visible gap before the box border.
- Given an ACL relationship, the line leaves the ACL box perpendicular to its outer edge (a visible horizontal or vertical stub before the curve bends) and enters the other context perpendicular to the context edge it meets.
- Given an ACL relationship, the arrow head appears at the upstream-context end of the line, pointing into that context. No arrow head appears at, or pointing at, the ACL box.
- All of the above hold for OHS relationships symmetrically, with the arrow head at the OHS-box end (pointing into the box).
- When the parent context of an indicator box is dragged so that the other context now lies on a different side of it, the indicator box relocates to that side and every other acceptance criterion above continues to hold (existing relocation behavior preserved).

NOT IN SCOPE:
- Changing the curve style of plain relationship edges that have no ACL or OHS indicator box. This refinement is scoped to ACL/OHS edges only.
- Changing the indicator label, colors, or hover/tooltip behavior. See contextflow-st9 for tooltip layering.
- Two-sided relationship pattern modeling. See contextflow-ki1.
notes: Mockup: docs/mockups/acl-ohs-box-geometry-mockup.html. Three interactive demos (Demo 1 ACL side-attached, Demo 2 OHS side-attached, Demo 3 ACL auto-relocate around the destination context as either context is dragged). Toggle "Attachment edge" defaults to "Opposite from context" and "Curve style" defaults to "Tangent at edge"; these are the chosen target behavior.
close_reason: Implemented ACL/OHS indicator box geometry refinement per docs/mockups/acl-ohs-box-geometry-mockup.html. Box hugs parent at 2px gap, attachment at outer-edge midpoint, tangent-at-edge bezier perpendicular at both endpoints, arrow placement preserved at upstream end.

title: Context-mapping only mode (hide value stream/chain trappings)
description:
Add a mode that hides value stream/chain UI so the canvas shows only contexts and relationships - a pure context-mapping view.

What gets hidden in this mode:
- Users row
- User needs row
- Value stream stage names (column headers/labels)
- Value chain vertical (left axis labels)
- Blue section (the value stream backdrop/band)

What remains visible:
- Bounded contexts
- Relationships between contexts
- Groups (capability clusters) - TBD, probably yes since they are pure context-mapping concepts

Needs this serves:
1. Minimal context-mapping view for teaching/practicing basic context mapping in isolation, without users having to mentally filter out value stream concepts they have not learned yet
2. Possible first step toward building a full value stream context map - users can start with just the contexts and relationships, then progressively reveal the value stream/chain trappings as they need them
3. Open: other use cases TBD (e.g., simplified screenshots for blog posts/slides? read-only sharing of a context map without confusing audiences unfamiliar with value-stream framing? export/print mode?)

Open design questions:
- Is this a toggle (like Show Issue Labels) or a separate view alongside Flow/Strategic/Distillation?
- If toggle: where does it live (View menu, settings, keyboard shortcut)?
- Does it just hide chrome, or does it also disable interactions tied to the hidden elements (assigning users, editing stage names, etc.)?
- How does it interact with positions? Contexts keep their flow.x positions but the visual scaffolding is gone - do we want any repositioning, or just hide overlays?
- Persistence: per-project setting, per-user preference, or session-only?
- Naming: 'Context Map mode'? 'Simple mode'? 'Teaching mode'?

Prior art / related:
- See VISION.md for the relationship between context mapping and value stream framing
- See UX_GUIDELINES.md for how progressive disclosure is handled elsewhere
notes:
## Separate view vs. toggle: persona analysis

### Personas

**DDD trainer / workshop facilitator** (Paul, Alberto, other teachers)
- Wants: project a clean context map during the context-mapping module, zero value-stream chrome
- Needs: high-discoverability, one-action switch, audience-nameable ('we are in Context Map view')
- Leans: separate view (workflow mode for whole teaching segment)

**First-time learner / workshop participant**
- Wants: not to be overwhelmed by users, needs, stages, value chain before meeting those concepts
- Needs: clear 'this is what context mapping is' framing with sensible on-ramp later
- Leans: separate view; the name itself teaches

**Practitioner building a real value stream context map progressively**
- Wants: start with contexts/relationships, reveal stages, users/needs, value chain as model fills in
- Needs: low-friction toggling, no loss of positions/state
- Leans: toggle on Flow View; revealing layers, not switching modes

**Strategist / architect sharing a map**
- Wants: clean export or shared link for an audience unfamiliar with value-stream framing
- Needs: possibly a render-time option (clean PNG/PDF) rather than an in-app mode
- Leans: neither; export option

### Tradeoffs

| | Separate view | Toggle on Flow View |
|---|---|---|
| Discoverability for trainers | High | Lower |
| Naming teaches the concept | Yes | Weaker |
| Progressive reveal for practitioners | Awkward | Natural |
| Shareable state | Yes | Harder |
| Cognitive load on view switcher | Adds 4th view | None |
| Coordinate/position questions | Must answer | Just hides overlays |

### Tentative direction

Trainer/learner use case is stronger; a named view does more pedagogical work than a toggle. Progressive-reveal need can be addressed inside that view with layered toggles (show stages, show users, show value chain) rather than treating the whole thing as one big filter on Flow View. Collapses both needs into one place without fragmenting Flow View.

Open: does the strategist export need warrant a separate render-time mode, or is 'enter Context Map view and screenshot' sufficient?

title: Fix joint side selection for floating edge anchors (GH #21)
description: Bug: getEdgeParams in src/lib/edgeGeometry.ts selects source and target anchor sides independently, producing wrap-around bezier curves when contexts are diagonally positioned (e.g., Top of source paired with Left of target). Replace with joint optimization: enumerate all 16 (sourceSide, targetSide) combinations, compute distance between side midpoints, pick the minimum. See GH issue #21 for details and the Dome Safety -> Central Control example. Display-geometry fix only; no change to creation UX or relationship model. Bezier paths preserved.

title: Slice 8: migrate old relationship pattern values (Phase 2 cleanup)
description: Slice 8 of contextflow-ki1. User behavior: existing projects with old-format pattern values continue to work and quietly migrate to the new model. Built-in example projects render correctly without runtime migration. Separate Ways becomes a deletion. Shared Kernel becomes a SharedKernel entity (per contextflow-cqi). Pattern type narrows from 8 values to 2 (customer-supplier, partnership). See docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md (Slice 8). Ships under no pressure once Slices 1-7 land. Concentrates the breaking-change risk.

title: Support two-sided relationship patterns on context map connections
description:
GH #13. Replace the single 8-value relationship pattern with a two-sided model: a relationship is characterized either by a standalone pattern (Customer-Supplier, Partnership, Shared Kernel) OR by per-side roles (upstream: OHS / Published Language; downstream: Conformist / ACL). Tom Asel top priority for workshop prep.

Scope: Phase 1 (Slices 1-7 in docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md). Phase 2 migration (Slice 8) is deferred and ships under no pressure.

Canonical references:
- docs/mockups/two-sided-relationships-target.html, implementation target, annotated with slice badges. This is what the implementer reads.
- docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md, slice plan, eight user-value slices with BDD acceptance criteria.
- docs/mockups/drag-direction-options-mockup.html, design discovery record showing alternatives evaluated. Read for rationale only.
- docs/DDD_RELATIONSHIP_PATTERNS.md, validity matrix sourced from Evans.
- docs/PLAN-two-sided-relationships.md, original v1 plan, superseded by v2 but preserved.

Observable acceptance criteria (Phase 1): the per-slice scenarios in PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md. Each slice ships as its own PR with the scenarios as new integration tests.

The implementer reads the target mockup for visual fidelity and the slice plan for what changes in this slice. If a behavior is in the mockup but not called out in the current slice, defer it to its assigned slice. Do not pull scaffolding from the design-discovery mockup.
notes:
Pre-resolved design decisions (do not re-litigate inside slices; full reasoning in the design-discovery mockup and the v2 plan):

1. Influence type is internal vocabulary (Evans, DDD Reference pp. 37-38): Upstream/Downstream, Mutually Dependent, Free. Derived from pattern + roles, never stored, never a picker control.
2. User picks concrete patterns, not influence types. Picker exposes Customer-Supplier, Partnership, and per-side roles (OHS, Published Language, Conformist, ACL).
3. Shared Kernel is not a picker option. It is created by the drag-to-overlap gesture (Slice 6). Picker shows a Currently: Shared Kernel banner when active.
4. Free has no relationship object. The user expresses Free by removing the relationship. No Free pill.
5. Separate Ways migration is a deletion, not a rename or conversion to blank (resolves Outstanding Question #1 from v1).
6. All pills toggle off when the active one is clicked. No separate clear links.
7. Mutual exclusivity is auto-resolved: picking a standalone pattern clears per-side roles and vice versa. Mutation layer is permissive; UI prevents invalid states.
8. Drag-stub gesture sets U/D direction: drag-from is downstream, drag-to is upstream, arrow points to upstream. Preserves P1 from contextflow-twa (#22).
9. Direction flip has three surfaces: picker mini-diagram (eta, Slice 4), canvas double-click (epsilon, Slice 5), and the existing right-click context menu. All three share the swapRelationshipDirection store action.
10. Flip clears per-side roles; preserves Customer-Supplier. Slice 4 extends swapRelationshipDirection so that on every flip, upstreamRole and downstreamRole are cleared while pattern is left untouched. Reason: per-side roles are claims about a specific context (e.g. OHS on Billing means Billing has built an Open Host Service) and should not silently migrate to the other context when direction changes. Customer-Supplier is a relationship-level pattern whose supplier/customer labels are defined by direction, so flipping just relabels who plays which role. Because all three flip surfaces share the action, the right-click context menu inherits the new behavior automatically. See PLAN-two-sided-relationships-v2.md pre-resolved decision 10 for worked examples.

Open decisions a worker should NOT silently make:
- Outstanding Question #2 (project-level schemaVersion field): still unresolved. Out of scope for this bead. Flag if it blocks Phase 2 planning, do not add the field here.

Slice beads (filed as separate work items):
- contextflow-bwn: Slice 1 (per-side roles in inspector). Done.
- contextflow-h82: Slice 2 (edge indicators for per-side roles). Done.
- contextflow-7am: Slice 3 (picker restructure). Done.
- contextflow-1w4: Slice 4 (eta direction control in picker + extend swapRelationshipDirection). Blocked by Slice 3.
- contextflow-2v6: Slice 5 (epsilon double-click on canvas). Blocked by Slice 4.
- contextflow-384: Slice 6 (drag-overlap creates Shared Kernel). Blocked by contextflow-cqi.
- contextflow-hm4: Slice 7 (drag-apart auto-creates Partnership). Blocked by Slice 6 and contextflow-cqi.
- contextflow-jf0: Slice 8 (migrate old pattern values). Blocked by Slices 1, 2, 3, 6, 7 and contextflow-cqi.

Related open beads (coordinate, do not absorb):
- contextflow-cqi: Shared Kernel as overlapping contexts with no connecting line. Slices 6, 7, 8 depend on cqi.
- contextflow-b6a: ACL/OHS indicator-box collision when one context plays both roles on the same side. Pure layout bug; Slice 2 increased the chance of triggering it but does not resolve it.
- contextflow-092: first-run coach mark for the connection gesture. Direction-teaching wording lands there once the picker direction control (Slice 4) exists.

Sequencing:
- Slices 1-5 are independent of cqi and ship sequentially as separate PRs.
- Slices 6-7 wait for cqi.
- Slice 8 (migration) ships last, under no pressure.

ki1 itself is the umbrella; it can be closed when all slice beads close.

title: Track group membership changes
description:
Group creation and deletion are tracked (group_created, group_deleted) but membership changes are not.

Store actions with no analytics (store.ts):
- removeContextFromGroup (line 528)
- addContextToGroup (line 533)
- addContextsToGroup (line 538)

All three just call getCollabMutations() with no tracking.

Fix: Add context_added_to_group and context_removed_from_group events. Include member_count (number of contexts in group after the change). Do NOT include group names or context names (PII policy). Use hashProjectId pattern if group_id is needed.

title: Redesign Context Inspector panel layout
description:
GH #15. Reorganize the ContextInspector layout into 7 visual groups. No store / type / mutation changes. No enum value changes (Role display labels are abbreviated for pill fit; underlying enum values are preserved). One small behavioral nuance is intentional: Boundary Notes textarea becomes conditional (see section 4).

## Section hierarchy

Reorganize ~20 flat sections into 7 visual groups separated by subtle dividers:
1. Identity (no header): Name, Purpose, Connected Users
2. Strategic Profile: Classification + Evolution badges (single row, with Domain / Evolution mini-labels above), Business Model Role pill group
3. Team & Organization: Ownership pill group, Team dropdown (hidden when ownership=external), Groups chips
4. Codebase: Repos cards, Code Size pill group, Boundary Integrity pill group + Boundary Notes textarea (BEHAVIORAL CHANGE: textarea is currently always rendered; in the redesign it renders only when boundaryIntegrity is set), Legacy + Big Ball of Mud toggles in a 2-col grid
5. Notes & Issues: Notes textarea, Issues list
6. Relationships: Upstream / Downstream / Mutual + Add Relationship
7. Danger Zone (no header): Delete Context

Temporal Position section continues to render after Strategic Profile when viewMode=strategic and project.temporal.enabled and currentDate is set (current behavior at ContextInspector.tsx:346, preserve as-is).

## Pill groups (replace 4 enum selects)

Enum fields use pill groups, not dropdowns. Team stays a dropdown (user-defined list, not a fixed enum).

| Field | Options (enum value -> display label) | Layout | Active variant |
|---|---|---|---|
| Ownership | ours -> Our Team, internal -> Internal, external -> External | horizontal flex, flex-1 each | green |
| Role | revenue-generator -> Revenue, engagement-creator -> Engagement, compliance-enforcer -> Compliance, cost-reduction -> Cost Reduction | grid-cols-2 (2x2) | green |
| Code Size | tiny / small / medium / large / huge (capitalized) | grid-cols-3 (3+2) | slate |
| Boundary | weak -> Weak, moderate -> Moderate, strong -> Strong | grid-cols-3 | slate |

Enum values are unchanged; only display labels for Role are abbreviated (e.g. "Revenue Generator" -> "Revenue") for pill fit. The stored businessModelRole value continues to be revenue-generator etc.

## PillGroup component shape

In src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx, add a generic PillGroup that accepts:
- options: array of { value, label, adornment? } where adornment is an optional ReactNode rendered before the label
- value: current selection (or undefined)
- onChange(newValue)
- layout: horizontal | grid-2 | grid-3
- variant: green | slate (controls active background, text, and ring color)

Boundary uses the adornment slot for a 14x10px border swatch (border-style preview); Code Size uses it for a scaled square dot (6px Tiny -> 8 Small -> 10 Medium -> 12 Large -> 14 Huge).

## Pill styling (Tailwind)

Base: px-2 py-1 text-[11px] font-medium rounded
Inactive: bg-slate-100 text-slate-600 dark:bg-neutral-800 dark:text-slate-400
Active (green): bg-green-100 text-green-800 ring-1 ring-green-400 dark:bg-green-900/40 dark:text-green-300 dark:ring-green-600
Active (slate): bg-slate-200 text-slate-900 ring-1 ring-slate-500 dark:bg-neutral-700 dark:text-slate-100 dark:ring-neutral-500
Hover (inactive): bg-slate-200 dark:bg-neutral-700

## Field labels and help icons (preserve existing idiom)

Each pill field has a label row above the pill group: text-xs text-slate-500 dark:text-slate-400 mb-1, followed by the existing InfoTooltip help icon (preserve the established pattern at ContextInspector.tsx:193, :443, :464):

  <InfoTooltip content={...} position="bottom">
    <HelpCircle size={14} className="text-slate-400 dark:text-slate-500 cursor-help" />
  </InfoTooltip>

Field-level tooltip content (unchanged from current code):
- Ownership: no existing field-level tooltip in current code; do not add one in this bead (per-pill tooltips deferred to contextflow-939)
- Role: BUSINESS_MODEL_ROLE concept
- Code Size: CODE_SIZE_TIERS concept
- Boundary: BOUNDARY_INTEGRITY[selectedValue] when set (current behavior; preserve)

## Other shared components

Add to src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx:
- SectionDivider: a wrapper div with border-t border-slate-200 dark:border-neutral-700 mt-4 pt-4 that contains the next section
- FIELD_LABEL_CLASS constant for the consistent inline field label styling above
- SELECT_CLASS constant retained for the Team picker

## Files

- src/components/inspector/inspectorShared.tsx: add PillGroup, SectionDivider, FIELD_LABEL_CLASS (existing exports unchanged)
- src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx: reorganize JSX per the 7-section hierarchy; replace Ownership / Role / Code Size / Boundary controls with PillGroup; move classification + evolution badges to a single row; move Legacy + Big Ball of Mud into a 2-col toggle grid; change Boundary Notes textarea to render only when boundaryIntegrity is set

## Acceptance criteria (observable)

A reviewer opens the app with a context that has many properties populated (e.g. the Payment Processing example in docs/inspector-panel-mockup.html) and:
1. Sees seven distinct visual groups separated by thin dividers, in the order above.
2. Sees Ownership, Role, Code Size, and Boundary rendered as pill groups (no native select dropdowns for those four fields).
3. Clicking a Boundary pill (Weak / Moderate / Strong) updates the selected context node border on the canvas to dotted-1.5px / solid-2px / solid-3px respectively.
4. Clicking a Code Size pill updates the selected context node width/height on the canvas to the corresponding NODE_SIZES bucket (canvasConstants.ts).
5. Clicking a Role pill updates businessModelRole to the matching enum value (verify by deselecting and reselecting the context; same pill is highlighted; exported project JSON contains the unabbreviated enum value e.g. "revenue-generator").
6. The Team picker remains a dropdown and continues to hide when ownership = external.
7. The Boundary Notes textarea appears only when a Boundary pill is selected; selecting Boundary reveals the textarea; clearing Boundary (no pill active) hides the textarea.
8. Toggles for Legacy and Big Ball of Mud render side-by-side in one row.
9. Temporal Position section appears after Strategic Profile in Strategic View when temporal mode is enabled with a current date.
10. Dark mode renders all pill states (inactive, hover, active green, active slate) with sufficient contrast (visually inspect with system dark mode on).

## Tooling gates

npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all pass with no new failures.

## References

- docs/inspector-panel-redesign.md (full plan)
- docs/inspector-panel-mockup.html (visual reference, all-pills variant in the "Proposed" column)
- src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts (BUSINESS_MODEL_ROLE, CODE_SIZE_TIERS, BOUNDARY_INTEGRITY used by existing field-level help tooltips)

title: Drop canvas pattern-selection popover; use picker as single source of truth
description:
Slice 3 gap. The bead contextflow-7am restructured the inspector picker and the create-relationship dialog but missed the third creation surface: the canvas drag-connector pattern-selection popover (CanvasArea.tsx "Create Relationship" dialog). That popover still showed the full eight legacy patterns including the four per-side values (OHS / PL / Conformist / ACL), so dragging a connector and picking Conformist created a relationship with pattern='conformist', which the new picker cannot surface as a pickable option.

Per the target mockup, the canvas drop should not need an intermediate popover. Drag → drop creates a no-pattern relationship and the inspector picker opens for configuration. This makes the picker the single source of truth for pattern selection across creation and editing.

Implementation:
- Remove pendingConnection state and the Create Relationship modal in CanvasArea.tsx
- onConnect for context→context: create a relationship with pattern=undefined, no roles, then select it to open the inspector
- addRelationship now returns the new relationship id so the caller can select it
- Remove unused imports (PATTERN_DEFINITIONS, POWER_DYNAMICS_ICONS, X icon) from CanvasArea.tsx

Acceptance:
- Dragging a connector between two contexts no longer shows a pattern-selection popover
- A new relationship is created with no pattern and no per-side roles
- The inspector opens automatically with the new relationship selected so the user can configure it via the Slice 3 picker

title: Track temporal mode toggle

The trackFTUEMilestone function defines first_relationship_added but it's never called. The other 3 FTUE milestones are all triggered from store.ts:

title: Add Big Ball of Mud flag to bounded contexts
description: Add an isBigBallOfMud boolean field to BoundedContext, similar to isLegacy. When enabled, shows a visual badge on the context node (like the Legacy badge). Represents Eric Evans' strategic design pattern: a system with mixed models and inconsistent boundaries that needs isolation. Add to Inspector as a toggle alongside the existing Legacy toggle. Include an InfoTooltip explaining what BBoM means and why it matters (signals need for isolation/decomposition), following the existing tooltip pattern.

title: Temporal/evolution view button is broken
description:
Clicking the temporal button on the strategic view does nothing. Was working earlier this week (visible in a LinkedIn screenshot). Regression.

## Technical Context

The toggle button in TopBar.tsx calls `toggleTemporalMode()` which delegates to `getCollabMutations().toggleTemporal()`. The Yjs mutation in keyframeMutations.ts calls `getTemporalMap(ydoc)` which returns `null` if the temporal property doesn't exist in the Yjs document, causing the toggle to fail silently.

**Likely root causes (investigate in order):**
1. `getTemporalMap()` returning null for projects that lack an initialized temporal object
2. The collab mutation chain breaking somewhere between store → collabStore → Yjs

**Key files:**
- `src/components/TopBar.tsx` - temporal toggle button (only in strategic view)
- `src/components/TimeSlider.tsx` - timeline UI, renders when `project?.temporal?.enabled`
- `src/model/store.ts` - `toggleTemporalMode()` action
- `src/model/sync/keyframeMutations.ts` - `toggleTemporalMutation()`, `getTemporalMap()`

**Note:** KeyframeManager.tsx and KeyframeEditor.tsx exist but are never imported anywhere in the codebase. May be dead code or incomplete wiring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking temporal toggle in strategic view enables the TimeSlider at the bottom of the canvas
- Toggling off hides the TimeSlider
- Works for both new and existing projects

title: Inspector panels: put name input on same row as close button to reclaim vertical space
description:
## Problem

Every inspector panel renders a dedicated row at the top just for the close (X) button, then a small vertical gap, then the name input as the first row of the inspector body. That dedicated row plus the gap wastes a noticeable strip of vertical space directly above the entity name on every panel (Context, Group, User, UserNeed, Team, FlowStage; Relationship and the connection inspectors also pay the same chrome cost).

This is most visible on a freshly created entity where only the name is set: the panel shows a band of empty space, then the name, then the rest of the panel.

## Source of the wasted space

`src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx:15-27` defines `CloseButton` as a `div` with `flex justify-end -mt-1 -mr-1 mb-2`. It is always rendered above whichever specific inspector is chosen (line 98), so every inspector inherits this fixed first row.

Each editable-name inspector then starts its own JSX with an `<input className={INPUT_TITLE_CLASS}>` as the first element. So we always pay: close-button row + `mb-2` gap + name row.

## Goal

Render the close (X) button on the same row as the entity name input, right-aligned. The name input grows to fill the row; the X sits to its right. No gap above the name.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Every inspector panel that currently shows an editable name (Context, Group, User, UserNeed, Team, FlowStage) renders the name input and the close button on a single horizontal row at the top of the panel, with no row above the name.
2. The close-button hit target stays roughly the same size as today (currently `p-1.5` around a 16px X icon); accessibility is preserved (`aria-label="Close inspector"`).
3. For inspectors without an editable name (Relationship, UserNeedConnection, NeedContextConnection), the close button still appears in the top-right corner, but no longer takes a whole row when something else can share it; if there is nothing else, an inspector-supplied header (e.g. a small label) sits beside it.
4. Visually, this should look like a tightened version of the existing layout, not a redesign. Same fonts, same spacing rhythm below the new top row.
5. The Delete button at the bottom of each inspector is untouched.

## Implementation options (open question; pick during implementation)

The chrome-vs-content split makes the right factoring nontrivial because the name input lives inside each inspector body today:

- **Slot approach**: `InspectorPanel` accepts a `topRow` (or `title`) slot from the chosen inspector and renders it on the same flex row as the X. Each inspector exports its name input via that slot. Cleanest separation, touches every inspector once.
- **Move close into each inspector**: each inspector renders its own top row with name + X. Duplication across inspectors, but no shared-API change. Probably the wrong tradeoff long-term.
- **Hoist the name into the panel**: have `InspectorPanel` own the name input itself, with a per-entity wiring layer. Bigger refactor; out of scope unless we also want to consolidate other shared chrome.

Recommend the slot approach. Smallest change to `InspectorPanel.tsx`, predictable update at the top of each inspector body, no behavioral changes.

## Files in scope

- `src/components/InspectorPanel.tsx` (replace `CloseButton` with a header row that accepts a left-side child)
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx`, `GroupInspector.tsx`, `UserInspector.tsx`, `UserNeedInspector.tsx`, `TeamInspector.tsx`, `FlowStageInspector.tsx` (lift the name input out of the body into the new header slot, or render at top with shared styling so it lines up)
- `src/components/inspector/RelationshipInspector.tsx`, `UserNeedConnectionInspector.tsx`, `NeedContextConnectionInspector.tsx` (no name to lift; confirm header still renders sanely with just the X, or expose a small label)
- Inspector tests in `src/components/inspector/__tests__/` (mostly query `getByDisplayValue` for the name; layout change should not break those; confirm)

## Out of scope

- Reordering content within an inspector body.
- Restyling the close-button glyph or hover behavior.
- Any logic around when the panel opens or closes.

Raise the ceiling significantly (e.g., 4-8x) so users can zoom in close to inspect a single context, read small text, or work in dense areas of the map. Also consider whether minZoom (currently 0.1) needs adjustment for very large maps. Verify overlay cutoffs (issue labels, team labels at zoom < 0.4) and node rendering still look reasonable at the new bounds.

title: Bug: dragging contexts in Strategic/Distillation views does not auto-classify
description: Moving a context in Strategic view should auto-update evolutionStage (genesis/custom-built/product/commodity) based on x position. Moving in Distillation view should auto-update strategicClassification (core/supporting/generic). The logic exists in contextActions.ts moveContextAction (lines 113-126) using classifyFromStrategicPosition and classifyFromDistillationPosition, but this is dead code. The store's updateContextPosition just calls getCollabMutations().updateContextPosition() with no classification. The distillation view has partial logic in setViewMode (runs on view switch only, not during drag). Fix: add auto-classification to the collab mutation code path, likely in updateContextPosition or as a post-drag handler similar to completeFlowStageMove.

title: Context node hover tooltip should appear instantly, not after 500ms
description:
## Symptom

All tooltips in the app should appear instantly on hover. Two sources of delay currently violate that:

1. The context-node hover tooltip uses a 500ms `setTimeout` before showing.
2. Nine controls use the native HTML `title="..."` attribute, which has a browser-imposed delay (~700ms-1500ms depending on OS) and looks inconsistent with SimpleTooltip / InfoTooltip elsewhere. CLAUDE.md explicitly prohibits `title` for this reason.

## Sources

**500ms timer:**
- `src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx:21` (`tooltipTimerRef`)
- `src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx:165` (`setTimeout(... 500)`)
- mouseLeave / mouseDown handlers that clear the timer (lines around 178)

**Native `title=` attributes:**
- `src/components/KeyframeManager.tsx:126` Jump to keyframe
- `src/components/KeyframeManager.tsx:133` Duplicate keyframe
- `src/components/KeyframeManager.tsx:140` Delete keyframe
- `src/components/ProjectListContent.tsx:238` Rename project
- `src/components/ProjectListContent.tsx:245` Duplicate project
- `src/components/ProjectListContent.tsx:253` Delete project
- `src/components/ProjectListContent.tsx:320` Duplicate project
- `src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx:248` Legacy
- `src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx:264` Big Ball of Mud

Note: the StepCard `title=` usages in `GettingStartedGuideModal.tsx` and `ValueChainGuideModal.tsx` are component props (heading labels rendered as text), NOT native HTML title attributes. Leave those alone.

## Expected

Every tooltip in the app appears instantly on hover, matching SimpleTooltip / InfoTooltip behavior. Mouse-leave still hides immediately. The `showHelpTooltips` setting still gates the context-node tooltip.

## Fix

1. ContextNode.tsx: remove the `setTimeout` and `tooltipTimerRef`. Call `setShowTooltip(true)` directly inside the `if (showHelpTooltips)` block on mouseEnter. Drop the `clearTimeout` calls in mouseLeave and mouseDown.
2. Replace each native `title="..."` attribute with `SimpleTooltip` wrapping the same trigger element. SimpleTooltip already displays instantly.

## Out of scope

- Restyling tooltip content or layout.
- Changing what content getContextTooltipLines returns.
- Modifying SimpleTooltip / InfoTooltip themselves.
- Adding new tooltip types or affordances.

title: Separate identity icons from attention counters on context cards
description:
## Problem

Two kinds of information live on a context card and look the same today:

- **Identity** (External pill, Legacy icon, Big Ball of Mud icon) — static facts about the context, set during modeling.
- **Attention** (Issue annotations: info, warning, critical) — things workshop participants or engineers should look at.

Mixing them in one row of icons reads as a sea of warnings. A workshop attendee sees four orange triangles and thinks "everything is broken" when one of them just means "this is legacy code." On top of that, the External pill double-encodes ownership (dashed border + text label) and overlaps the name on long titles, and multiple top-right badges (Legacy, BBoM, issues) collide when more than two are present.

## Design (see docs/mockups/context-node-badge-layout-mockup.html)

**The mockup is the visual source of truth.** Lift exact CSS values (colors, sizes, spacing, shadows, backdrop filters, font sizes, font-variant) directly from the mockup file rather than reinventing them. The notes below describe behavior and intent; the mockup describes pixels.

### External: drop the pill, lean on the border
- Remove the "EXTERNAL" text pill entirely.
- External contexts get a **dashed border + slate fill** (`#e2e8f0` background, `#64748b` dashed border). The visual cue carries the signal.
- The existing whole-card hover tooltip (`getContextTooltipLines` in `src/lib/contextTooltip.ts`) names ownership when `colorByMode === "ownership"`. In `colorByMode === "strategic"` it does not — so external strategic-colored cards rely on the dashed border alone for ownership signal. Broader fix tracked in contextflow-g7l.

### Identity icons (Legacy, BBoM): top-right corner, single flex container
- One absolutely-positioned flex container at `top: 4px; right: 4px;` with `display: inline-flex; gap: 4px;` holds both icons.
- **Reading order (left to right): Legacy, BBoM**. So BBoM sits at the card edge and Legacy is to its left. This is a deliberate flip from today, where Legacy is at the right edge and BBoM is to its left. The new arrangement matches the mockup and removes the per-icon `right: 4 / right: 24` math.
- No "EXTERNAL" pill in this zone; only the two identity icons.

### Name: top of card, single line, no margin-top
- Sits at the top-left of the card. Never gets pushed down.
- Gets `padding-right` only when identity icons are present: **24px for one icon, 44px for two**.
- **Single line only**: apply `white-space: nowrap` so the name truncates with ellipsis rather than wrapping to multiple lines. The current code is missing `white-space: nowrap`, so long names wrap today; that needs to change.
- The d8r `nameMarginTop: 14` fix is removed; issues no longer live at top-left, so the marginTop hack is no longer needed.

### Issues: per-severity counters at bottom-right
- Absolutely positioned at `right: 6px; bottom: 6px;`.
- VS Code / Sentry pattern: small counters showing icon + count, one per non-zero severity.
- Order: critical → warning → info.
- Each counter keeps its severity color.
- Wrapped in a single rounded pill with a semi-transparent white background and a subtle blur, so it reads against any card fill color. **See mockup CSS for exact values** (background opacity, blur radius, box-shadow, icon size, font size, gap, padding, tabular-nums).
- Same format on all card sizes including tiny.
- **Tooltip lists the issues by severity** (each issues title, grouped under its severity heading). Example:
  ```
  Critical (1)
    • Payment timeout under load
  Warnings (2)
    • Concurrency hotspot
    • Slow query on order_items
  ```
  The whole counter pill carries one combined tooltip; individual per-counter tooltips are not needed.
- Clicking the counter pill selects the context (so the inspector opens to it). Selecting the context already shows its issue list in the inspector today; no new inspector behavior is needed.

## Card size hierarchy (from src/lib/canvasConstants.ts)

| Bucket  | Width | Height | Description shown? |
|---------|-------|--------|--------------------|
| tiny    | 120   | 70     | hidden             |
| small   | 140   | 80     | hidden             |
| medium  | 170   | 100    | visible            |
| large   | 200   | 150    | visible            |
| huge    | 240   | 240    | visible            |

The per-severity counter format must fit in the **120×70 tiny** card at minimum. Verify in the browser, not just in the mockup (the mockup card labeled "tiny" was actually small-sized in the original sketch).

## Acceptance criteria

1. External + long name: no overlap between any chrome element and the name. External is signalled by a dashed border and slate fill; the EXTERNAL text pill is gone.
2. External + BBoM + issue: no overlap. BBoM icon is in the top-right corner; the issue counter pill is in the bottom-right corner.
3. Internal + Legacy + BBoM + multiple issues (mixed severity): Legacy sits to the left of BBoM in the top-right corner with 4px gap (BBoM on the card edge); per-severity issue counters sit in the bottom-right corner. No two elements overlap.
4. Long name + identity icons: the name renders on a single line and ends with an ellipsis when truncated. Identity icons remain fully visible.
5. No badges and no issues: the name sits at the top of the card with no reserved vertical space above it.
6. All card sizes from 120×70 (tiny) through 240×240 (huge): the issue counter pill renders fully inside the card border (not clipped) and does not overlap the name.
7. Hovering Legacy or BBoM with `showHelpTooltips` enabled shows the concept-definition tooltip (`LEGACY_CONTEXT` and `BIG_BALL_OF_MUD` from `src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts`). With `showHelpTooltips` disabled, no tooltip appears on these icons (matches existing InfoTooltip gating behaviour; a small regression from todays unconditional `SimpleTooltip`).
8. Hovering the issue counter pill shows a tooltip listing the issues grouped by severity, with each issues title.
9. Clicking the issue counter pill selects the underlying context (same behaviour as clicking the card body).

## Files in scope

- `src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx`
  - Remove the `External` pill block.
  - Remove `nameMarginTop` (the d8r fix) and the top-left issues block.
  - Add a new bottom-right per-severity issue-counter component.
  - Replace the two separate absolute-positioned Legacy and BBoM blocks with one absolute-positioned flex container holding both icons in `[Legacy, BBoM]` order.
  - For external contexts, change the background fill (currently orange `#fed7aa`) to slate (`#e2e8f0`).
  - Keep `topRightBadgeReserve` logic (24/44) — the math is unchanged; only the icon order inside the reserved space flips.
  - Add `white-space: nowrap` to the name to force single-line + ellipsis.
  - Swap `SimpleTooltip` for `InfoTooltip` on the Legacy and BBoM badges, passing `LEGACY_CONTEXT` and `BIG_BALL_OF_MUD` from `src/model/conceptDefinitions.ts`.
- `src/components/nodes/__tests__/ContextNode.issueIconLayout.test.tsx` (the d8r regression test) — replace with combination tests covering the new layout.
- Component tests in `src/components/nodes/__tests__/` — add scenarios for: long name + identity icons (single-line ellipsis), identity + issues, external + identity, external + issues, tiny card with issues at 120×70, counter tooltip listing issues by severity, counter click selecting the context, InfoTooltip on identity icons.

## Out of scope

- Restyling the severity colors themselves (critical red, warning orange, info blue stay as-is).
- Changing inspector UI for issues.
- IssueLabelsOverlay floating cards (separate feature).
- Changing ownership color for non-external contexts (the slate fill applies only to external).
- Making ownership visible in `colorByMode === "strategic"` — tracked in contextflow-g7l.
- Restoring an unconditional label tooltip on Legacy/BBoM when `showHelpTooltips` is off — if this regression matters, raise it separately.

## Mockup

`docs/mockups/context-node-badge-layout-mockup.html` shows the full design with current-vs-proposed comparisons, all combinations, and edge cases. Treat the CSS in that file as authoritative for pixel-level decisions.
notes:
## Design history

Earlier iterations of this bead proposed an explicit "header row" above the name. That approach was rejected during mockup review (2026-05-23) because it pushed the name down on every card, including the no-badges case. The final design keeps the name anchored at the top of the card with corner-anchored icons (identity top-right, issues bottom-right).

title: Excessive tooltip layering on relationship edge hover
description: Hovering a context relationship edge shows three overlapping affordances for the same fact: (1) the inline pattern label on the edge ("Partnership"), (2) a native browser <title> tooltip ("partnership") from <title>{pattern}</title> at src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx:328, (3) a custom portal-rendered educational tooltip (dark card with title, description, characteristics) at src/components/edges/RelationshipEdge.tsx:330-371. The native <title> also violates the project rule in CLAUDE.md ("NEVER use native title attribute"). Proposed fix: remove the <title> element entirely; keep the educational portal tooltip but only on first-time hover or when showHelpTooltips is on, and consider a short delay so it does not stack visually with the inline label. Alternatively, suppress the educational tooltip on hover when the edge already has a visible inline label, and reserve it for the indicator-box patterns (ACL/OHS) where the meaning is less obvious. Goal: one clear affordance per hover, not three.

title: [ux] Groups section chip looks like a static label but is a button
description:
## Symptom

In the Context inspector Groups section, the colored chip showing the group name is rendered as a `<button>` that navigates to the group (selects the group, deselects the context). Visually it reads as a static label / tag, not a clickable action, so users do not discover the navigation. Keyboard users tabbing through the inspector land on what feels like a "title" and are not sure what action it provides.

## Source

`src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx` around line 383:

```tsx
<button
  onClick={() =>
    useEditorStore.setState({
      selectedGroupId: group.id,
      selectedContextId: null,
    })
  }
  className="text-xs font-medium hover:underline"
  style={{ color: group.color || '#3b82f6' }}
>
  {group.label}
</button>
```

The affordance is a `hover:underline` only, so without hovering the user cannot tell it is interactive.

## Expected

The chip should look interactive. Possible directions to discuss before implementing:

1. Add a subtle persistent affordance: small chevron / link glyph next to the label, or constant underline on the label, plus a clearer hover state (background tint).
2. Add a focus-visible ring matching the keyboard-nav pattern used by PillGroup (contextflow-813), so keyboard users see they have landed on an actionable element.
3. Reconsider whether navigation should be the chip-click action at all; an alternative is a separate "open group" button on the chip and have the chip itself be non-interactive.

## Acceptance criteria

1. A user encountering the chip for the first time can tell at a glance that it is clickable, without hovering.
2. Tabbing to the chip shows a focus indicator that matches the rest of the inspector (`focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-blue-500 dark:focus-visible:ring-blue-400`).
3. Activating with mouse, Enter, or Space navigates to the group as before.
4. Visual change does not crowd the chip layout or conflict with the remove-X (covered by the sibling a11y bead).

## Notes

Related to [[contextflow-813]] (PillGroup keyboard nav) which set the focus-ring convention. Worth applying the same ring style here so the inspector has one consistent focus-visible style.
close_reason: Added persistent ArrowUpRight glyph, focus-visible ring, hover background tint, and aria-label so the chip reads as a navigation button without hovering. Keyboard activation works via native button semantics.

title: Phase-1 migration: legacy per-side pattern values to per-side roles
description:
Phase-1 (lightweight) migration to unblock Slice 3 picker usability. Existing projects can have pattern set to one of the four per-side values (open-host-service, published-language, conformist, anti-corruption-layer) from before the two-sided model. The new picker does not surface these as choices, so users cannot clear them; the Slice 2 fallback continues to render the ACL or OHS indicator box from pattern even after the user clears the corresponding per-side role pill. This is confusing in normal use.

Phase-1 scope (narrow, independent of contextflow-jf0):
- For each Relationship, if pattern is one of {open-host-service, published-language, conformist, anti-corruption-layer} and the matching per-side role (upstreamRole or downstreamRole) is unset, move the value to the role and set pattern to undefined.
- Idempotent (safe to re-run).
- Leaves shared-kernel and separate-ways untouched (those are Slice 8 / contextflow-jf0 territory).
- Built-in example projects load through the same migration path; no separate per-example edits needed.

Application points:
- src/model/migrations/ (new pure migration module + tests)
- Invoked from src/model/persistence.ts migrateProject for IndexedDB load
- Also applied when reading from Yjs (so multiplayer sessions see clean data) — exact wiring TBD during implementation

Acceptance:
- A relationship loaded with pattern: anti-corruption-layer migrates to downstreamRole: anti-corruption-layer, pattern: undefined
- A relationship loaded with pattern: anti-corruption-layer AND downstreamRole: anti-corruption-layer migrates pattern to undefined without changing the role
- A relationship loaded with pattern: anti-corruption-layer AND downstreamRole: conformist leaves both fields alone (do not overwrite a user-set role)
- shared-kernel, separate-ways, customer-supplier, partnership patterns are unchanged
- After migration, toggling a per-side role pill off no longer revives a ghost indicator box on the canvas.

title: Connection UX redesign: hover stubs, whole-shape drop targets (GH #22)
description:
Redesign relationship creation UX for the DDD Europe 2026 workshop audience. The current implementation renders two fixed source-handle dots on each context's left and right edges; users must precisely target them to start a connection, and the resulting edge's display geometry disagrees with where they dragged from. This bead replaces that with discoverable hover affordances and a forgiving drop target.

Three implementation changes:

1) Hover stubs (draw.io style). On hover, show directional arrow nubs on all 4 sides of a context (top, right, bottom, left). Nubs are translucent (~35% opacity) by default and pop to full opacity plus accent color on individual hover. A small tooltip appears on stub hover: "Drag to another context to map the relationship". Drag from any stub starts a connection. Replaces the existing fixed left/right source-handle dots.

2) Whole-shape drop target. While a connection-drag is in flight, dropping anywhere on the body of a destination context completes the connection. Implemented as a single transparent full-size React Flow target Handle behind the visible card so React Flow's existing hit-testing accepts the drop. The displayed endpoint floats to the closest side via the joint-anchor algorithm from #21. Body-drag of a card continues to move it: connection-drag and node-drag stay distinct because connection-drag is initiated by a stub mousedown, node-drag by a body mousedown.

3) Bezier curves preserved. The hand-drawn/organic feel is intentional for strategic-design work; orthogonal routing was considered and rejected. No change to path type.

Coupled deliverables that ship in the same PR:

- Arrow marker size: bump relationship arrow markers from markerWidth/markerHeight 5 to 8 (markers defined in src/components/CanvasArea.tsx around lines 1063-1119, all five marker variants). Reads better at workshop zoom levels.
- Rich card-hover tooltip guidance line: update the line in src/lib/contextTooltip.ts that currently reads "Drag handles to connect to other contexts" to "Drag any arrow to another context to map the relationship." This help text describes the changing affordance and will go stale if we ship the new affordance without updating it.

Key files to touch (orientation, not prescription): src/components/nodes/ContextNode.tsx (replace fixed source handles with hover stubs, add per-stub tooltip), src/components/CanvasArea.tsx (marker defs, onConnect flow), src/lib/contextTooltip.ts (guidance line wording), src/lib/edgeGeometry.ts (already correct via #21; no change expected). Existing User -> User Need -> Context flow uses the top target handle and must keep working.

Background and decisions: docs/connection-ux-research.md and docs/mockups/connection-ux-mockup.html. These currently live on the mockup/connection-ux branch (htmlpreview link on GH #22 comment); both will land in the implementation PR or its predecessor merge.

Direction convention is kept as-is for #22 (drag-from = downstream, drag-to = upstream; arrow points to upstream, as documented in src/model/types.ts:80-86). Reopen during two-sided pattern work (contextflow-ki1 / GH #13). All copy on the new creation surfaces is intentionally direction-neutral so symmetric patterns (Partnership, Shared Kernel, Separate Ways, which render with no arrow head) read correctly.

Acceptance criteria (observable behavior):
- Hovering a context reveals 4 translucent arrow nubs on top/right/bottom/left.
- Hovering one nub pops it to full opacity and shows tooltip "Drag to another context to map the relationship".
- Dragging from any nub starts a connection-drag with a ghost line following the cursor.
- Dropping on any point of a destination context's body (not just a handle) creates the relationship.
- Body-drag of a context (not on a nub) moves the context; no relationship is created.
- The displayed edge endpoint floats to the closest side per #21 (no regression).
- Relationship arrow markers render visibly larger than before (markerWidth/markerHeight 8 vs prior 5).
- The rich card-hover tooltip (gated by showHelpTooltips) shows updated guidance: "Drag any arrow to another context to map the relationship." No reference to "handles" remains.
- Existing User -> User Need -> Context connection flow still works.
- Symmetric patterns (Partnership, Shared Kernel, Separate Ways) still render with no arrow head.
- The original two fixed left/right source-handle dots are no longer rendered.

Separate decision flagged for the user (not in current scope):
- First-run coach mark teaching the gesture. This is an onboarding overlay, not a tooltip. Discussed during mockup review. Ask before pulling in.

See GH issue #22 for product framing and workshop motivation. Depends on contextflow-j7k (closed: joint anchor side selection for floating edges).
notes:
Mockup phase complete (2026-05-21). Branch: mockup/connection-ux. Htmlpreview link on GH #22 comment.

Memory entries to consult during implementation:
- design_bezier_edges.md (path type)
- design_arrow_marker_size.md (5 -> 8)
- design_drag_direction_convention.md (P1 kept for #22; revisit in ki1)
- feedback_visual_mockups_first.md (process)
- feedback_tooltips_ship_with_feature.md (why the rich tooltip update is in scope, not deferred)

Status: ready to start implementation (TDD). Outer-loop scenarios should follow the acceptance criteria list in the description.

title: Fix bezier line and arrow marker overlap at edge endpoint (GH #24)
description: The bezier path runs to the destination anchor on the box edge, and the arrow marker is drawn on top, causing line+marker visual overlap. Path should stop at the back of the arrow triangle so only the point touches the box. Two implementation options: (1) geometrically shorten the path by arrow length in RelationshipEdge.tsx or getEdgeParams, or (2) configure SVG marker refX to position the marker base at the path endpoint. Independent of #21 but sequencing-wise queue after #21. See GH #24.

title: Context inspector: panel scroll locks when an expanded repo card is open
description:
## Symptom

In the Context inspector, expanding a repo card (clicking the GitBranch chip in the Repos section) causes the inspector panel itself to stop scrolling. With the repo card collapsed, the inspector scrolls normally; with it expanded, the user cannot reach content above or below the visible viewport, even though there is clearly more panel content offscreen.

This blocks editing for any context that has both:
- A repo with non-trivial expanded content (contributor lists, stats, etc.), AND
- Content above or below that the user needs to reach (Notes textarea, Relationships, Delete button, name/purpose, etc.).

## Where to look

- The inspector panel host: `src/App.tsx:340` renders the right sidebar as `<aside className="... overflow-y-auto ...">` wrapping `<InspectorPanel />`. That overflow on the aside is the scroll surface.
- The repo card itself: `src/components/inspector/ContextRepoCard.tsx`. Toggled via the `expandedRepoId` state owned by `ContextInspector.tsx`. The expanded view renders contributor lists and stats; need to confirm none of those use a layout that clips or steals scroll events from the parent aside.
- `ContextInspector.tsx` lines 371-398 host the repo cards inside a `space-y-2` wrapper inside the Codebase `SectionDivider`. Need to check whether any ancestor has `overflow-hidden`, a fixed `height`, or a flex container with a missing `min-h-0` that would break the parent scroll.

## Likely suspects (verify before fixing)

- An expanded card with its own `overflow-hidden` or scrollable inner container, which can swallow wheel events in some browsers when the cursor is over the card.
- A nested flex/grid where the panel grows to fit content instead of overflowing, because a parent uses `h-full` without `min-h-0`.
- An `overflow-y-auto` accidentally set on the expanded repo content instead of letting the aside handle it.
- A mouse-wheel handler somewhere in the contributor list or stats sub-component that calls `stopPropagation`.

## Repro

1. Run `npm run dev:local`.
2. Open a project that has a context with at least one assigned repo, plus enough Notes + Relationships + Delete content to require panel scroll. (Any of the built-in samples should work.)
3. Select the context. Confirm the inspector panel scrolls normally.
4. Click the repo chip to expand it. Try to scroll the inspector. The panel no longer scrolls; the content above (name) and below (delete) is unreachable.
5. Collapse the repo. Panel scroll returns.

## Acceptance criteria

1. With a repo card expanded, the inspector panel still scrolls normally; the user can reach the name input at the top and the Delete button at the bottom by scrolling.
2. The expanded repo card may have its own internal scrolling for long contributor lists if desired, but it must not break the outer panel scroll.
3. Behavior is the same in light and dark mode, and in all current view modes (Flow / Strategic / Distillation).
4. No regression to the collapsed repo card layout.

## Files in scope (starting points)

- `src/components/inspector/ContextRepoCard.tsx`
- `src/components/inspector/ContextInspector.tsx`
- `src/App.tsx` (the aside that owns the scroll surface)

## Notes

This may share a root cause with contextflow-pmt (the inspector chrome / scroll surface is a small but real surface for layout bugs). Worth landing both together if the fix touches the same lines.
